revision.text:
{{日本の古墳| 名称=雷電山古墳| 所在地=埼玉県行東松山市大谷| 位置 = {{ウィキ座標2段度分秒|36|5|13.8|N|139|23|17.8|E|region:JP|display=inline,title}}| 形状=帆立貝形古墳| 規模=全長85m、高さ7m、後円部径73m、前方部幅39m| 築造年代=5世紀前半| 出土品=埴輪| 史跡指定=[[1956年]]市指定| }}
'''雷電山古墳'''（らいでんやまこふん）は、[[埼玉県]][[東松山市]]の三千塚古墳群内にある[[帆立貝形古墳]]である。埴輪を持つ埼玉県最古の古墳。
==概要== 大谷の丘陵上、標高90メートルの雷電山山頂に築造された。後円部の最上段のみ盛り土がされ、それ以外の部分は地山を削り出して造成されている。墳丘の形状は奈良・[[乙女山古墳]]に類似する。1984年の調査で、墳丘は三段構成であること、前方部頂部に葺石が施されていること、四重の埴輪列が巡ることが明らかになった。埼玉県で最も古い埴輪の出土例である。後円部墳頂にも方形の埴輪列が確認されており、その中心に埋葬施設が存在すると思われる。主体部は粘土槨か木棺直葬と見られている。1956(昭和31)年に市の史跡に指定。出土した埴輪も2004(平成16)年に市の重要文化財(考古資料)に指定されている。
==出土品== 朝顔型埴輪、円筒埴輪、器台型埴輪が確認された。これらの埴輪は、一般に知られているそれとは別物ともいえる特異なものである。いずれも表面の調整にハケメ工具が用いられておらず、これは形状が特異なばかりでなく、埴輪というよりむしろ土師器に近い技術で作られているといえる。野焼き製作を示す黒斑が認められることから5世紀前半の製作と推定されている。これらの埴輪は、現在埼玉県立歴史と民族の博物館に展示されている。
== 参考文献 == * 塩野博 「埼玉の古墳 比企・秩父」 さきたま出版会、2004年
== 関連項目 == *[[日本の古墳一覧]] *[[埼玉県の古墳一覧]]
== 外部リンク == *[http://kofun.info/kofun/312 雷電山古墳] - 古墳マップ
{{japanese-history-stub}} {{デフォルトソート:らいてんやまこふん}} [[Category:埼玉県の古墳]] [[Category:東松山市の歴史]]

{{単一の出典|date=2013年11月7日 (木) 17:00 (UTC)}} {{日本の古墳| 名称=雷電山古墳| 所属=[[三千塚古墳群]]| 所在地=埼玉県行東松山市大谷| 位置 = {{ウィキ座標2段度分秒|36|5|13.8|N|139|23|17.8|E|region:JP|display=inline,title}}| 形状=帆立貝形古墳| 規模=全長85m、高さ7m、後円部径73m、前方部幅39m| 築造年代=5世紀前半| 出土品=埴輪| 史跡指定=[[1956年]]市指定| }}
'''雷電山古墳'''（らいでんやまこふん）は、[[埼玉県]][[東松山市]]の[[三千塚古墳群]]内にある[[帆立貝形古墳]]である。[[埴輪]]を持つ埼玉県最古の古墳。
==概要== 大谷の丘陵上、標高90メートルの雷電山山頂に築造された。後円部の最上段のみ盛り土がされ、それ以外の部分は地山を削り出して造成されている。墳丘の形状は奈良・[[乙女山古墳]]に類似する。1984年の調査で、墳丘は三段構成であること、前方部頂部に[[葺石]]が施されていること、四重の埴輪列が巡ることが明らかになった。埼玉県で最も古い埴輪の出土例である。後円部墳頂にも方形の埴輪列が確認されており、その中心に埋葬施設が存在すると思われる。主体部は[[粘土槨]]か木棺直葬と見られている。1956(昭和31)年に「三千塚古墳群」として市の史跡に指定。出土した埴輪も2004(平成16)年に市の重要文化財(考古資料)に指定されている。
==出土品== [[朝顔形埴輪]]、[[円筒埴輪]]、器台型埴輪が確認された。これらの埴輪は、一般に知られているそれとは別物ともいえる特異なものである。いずれも表面の調整にハケメ工具が用いられておらず、これは形状が特異なばかりでなく、埴輪というよりむしろ[[土師器]]に近い技術で作られているといえる。野焼き製作を示す黒斑が認められることから5世紀前半の製作と推定されている。これらの埴輪は、現在[[埼玉県立歴史と民俗の博物館]]に展示されている。
== 参考文献 == {{参照方法|date=2013年11月7日 (木) 17:00 (UTC)}} * 塩野博 『埼玉の古墳 比企・秩父』 pp67-75、さきたま出版会、2004年
== 関連項目 == *[[日本の古墳一覧]] *[[埼玉県の古墳一覧]]

== 概要 == 大谷の丘陵上、標高90メートルの雷電山山頂に築造された。後円部の最上段のみ盛り土がされ、それ以外の部分は地山を削り出して造成されている。墳丘の形状は奈良・[[乙女山古墳]]に類似する。1984年（昭和59年）の調査で、墳丘は三段構成であること、前方部頂部に[[葺石]]が施されていること、四重の埴輪列が巡ることが明らかになった。埼玉県で最も古い埴輪の出土例である。後円部墳頂にも方形の埴輪列が確認されており、その中心に埋葬施設が存在すると思われる。主体部は[[粘土槨]]か木棺直葬と見られている。
古墳群は1956年（昭和31年）に「三千塚古墳群」として市の[[史跡]]に指定され、出土した埴輪も2004年（平成16年）に市の[[有形文化財]]（考古資料）に指定されている。
[[増田一郎]]は、突如現れる[[埼玉古墳群]]の故地を[[比企]]の地に比定しており、雷電山古墳は[[乎獲居臣]]の祖父である半弖比の墓であるとした<ref>中村倉司「埼玉丸慕山古墳と大里甲山古墳 ー武蔵国造家内紛と大型円墳一[https://sakitama-muse.spec.ed.jp/cabinets/cabinet_files/download/308/9407c74bd90fc866401e895534feb62b?frame_id=338]」</ref>。
== 出土品 == [[ファイル:雷電山古墳 (東松山市) 出土 円筒埴輪.JPG|thumb|180px|right|{{center|円筒埴輪}}{{small|[[埼玉県立歴史と民俗の博物館]]展示。}}]] [[朝顔形埴輪]]、[[円筒埴輪]]、器台形埴輪が確認された。これらの埴輪は、一般に知られているそれとは別物ともいえる特異なものである。いずれも表面の調整にハケメ工具が用いられておらず、これは形状が特異なばかりでなく、埴輪というよりむしろ[[土師器]]に近い技術で作られているといえる。野焼き製作を示す黒斑が認められることから5世紀前半の製作と推定されている。これらの埴輪は、現在[[埼玉県立歴史と民俗の博物館]]に展示されている。
==脚注== {{Reflist}}
== 参考文献 == {{参照方法|date=2013年11月7日 (木) 17:00 (UTC)|section=3}} * 塩野博『埼玉の古墳 比企・秩父』 pp.67-75、さきたま出版会、2004年

'''雷電山古墳'''（らいでんやまこふん）は、[[埼玉県]][[東松山市]]の[[三千塚古墳群]]内にある[[帆立貝形古墳]]である。[[埴輪]]を持つ埼玉県最古の古墳。
== 概要 == 大谷の丘陵上、標高90メートルの雷電山山頂に築造された。後円部の最上段のみ盛り土がされ、それ以外の部分は地山を削り出して造成されている。墳丘の形状は奈良・[[乙女山古墳]]に類似する。1984年（昭和59年）の調査で、墳丘は三段構成であること、前方部頂部に[[葺石]]が施されていること、四重の埴輪列が巡ることが明らかになった。埼玉県で最も古い埴輪の出土例である。後円部墳頂にも方形の埴輪列が確認されており、その中心に埋葬施設が存在すると思われる。主体部は[[粘土槨]]か木棺直葬と見られている。
古墳群は1956年（昭和31年）に「三千塚古墳群」として市の[[史跡]]に指定され、出土した埴輪も2004年（平成16年）に市の[[有形文化財]]（考古資料）に指定されている。
[[増田一郎]]は、突如現れる[[埼玉古墳群]]の故地を[[比企]]の地に比定しており、雷電山古墳は[[乎獲居臣]]の祖父である半弖比の墓であるとした<ref>中村倉司「埼玉丸慕山古墳と大里甲山古墳 ー武蔵国造家内紛と大型円墳一[https://sakitama-muse.spec.ed.jp/cabinets/cabinet_files/download/308/9407c74bd90fc866401e895534feb62b?frame_id=338]」</ref>。
== 出土品 == [[ファイル:雷電山古墳 (東松山市) 出土 円筒埴輪.JPG|thumb|180px|right|{{center|円筒埴輪}}{{small|[[埼玉県立歴史と民俗の博物館]]展示。}}]] [[朝顔形埴輪]]、[[円筒埴輪]]、器台形埴輪が確認された。これらの埴輪は、一般に知られているそれとは別物ともいえる特異なものである。いずれも表面の調整にハケメ工具が用いられておらず、これは形状が特異なばかりでなく、埴輪というよりむしろ[[土師器]]に近い技術で作られているといえる。野焼き製作を示す黒斑が認められることから5世紀前半の製作と推定されている。これらの埴輪は、現在[[埼玉県立歴史と民俗の博物館]]に展示されている。

revision.text:
{{Infobox tumulus | name = Raidenyama Kofun | image = {{Image requested|cat=Higashimatsuyama}} | belonging_to = {{ill|Sanzenzuka Kofun Cluster|ja|三千塚古墳群}} | location = {{ill|Ōya, Higashimatsuyama|ja|東松山市大谷}}, {{ill|Higashimatsuyama|ja|東松山市}}, {{ill|Saitama Prefecture|ja|埼玉県}}, Japan | coordinates = {{Coord|36|05|13.8|N|139|23|17.8|E|region:JP|display=inline,title}} | shape = {{ill|Scallop-shaped tumulus|ja|帆立貝形古墳}} | size = Total length 85m, height 7m, rear circle diameter 73m, front width 39m | construction_era = Early 5th century | artifacts = {{ill|haniwa|ja|埴輪}} | historic_site_designation = Included in Sanzenzuka Kofun Cluster; designated as a municipal historic site on February 6, 1956<ref name="Higashimatsuyama">{{Cite web|url=http://www.city.higashimatsuyama.lg.jp/kurashi/bunka_sports/bunka/bunkazai/shitei_bunkazai/1350459239838.html|title=List of Cultural Properties|publisher=Higashimatsuyama City|date=2021-07-02|accessdate=2021-11-29}}</ref> | tangible_cultural_properties = Excavated haniwa, designated on March 26, 2004<ref name="Higashimatsuyama"/> | map = {{Location map|Japan Saitama|label=Raidenyama Kofun|lat_deg=36|lat_min=05|lat_sec=13.8|lon_deg=139|lon_min=23|lon_sec=17.8|mark=帆立貝形古墳-北西.svg|marksize=28|float=center|border=none|position=bottom|width=200|caption=Location in Saitama Prefecture}} }}
'''Raidenyama Kofun''' (雷電山古墳) is a {{ill|scallop-shaped tumulus|ja|帆立貝形古墳}} located in the {{ill|Sanzenzuka Kofun Cluster|ja|三千塚古墳群}} in {{ill|Higashimatsuyama|ja|東松山市}}, {{ill|Saitama Prefecture|ja|埼玉県}}. It is the oldest tumulus in Saitama Prefecture known to feature {{ill|haniwa|ja|埴輪}}.
== Overview == Constructed atop the 90-meter-high summit of Raidenyama Hill in Ōya, the tumulus was built with earth heaped only on the uppermost tier of the rear circle, while other parts were carved from the natural terrain. The tumulus shape is similar to the {{ill|Otomeyama Kofun|ja|乙女山古墳}} in Nara Prefecture. Excavation in 1984 revealed a three-tiered mound structure, with paving stones on the summit of the front section and four concentric rows of haniwa encircling the tumulus. These haniwa are the oldest found in Saitama Prefecture. A square arrangement of haniwa was also discovered on the rear summit, suggesting the presence of a burial facility beneath. The burial facility is presumed to be either a clay-enclosed burial chamber or a wooden coffin burial.
The site was designated as part of the "Sanzenzuka Kofun Cluster" municipal historic site in 1956, and the excavated haniwa were designated as municipal tangible cultural properties in 2004.
Isao Masuda hypothesized that the origin of the {{ill|Sakitama Kofun Cluster|ja|埼玉古墳群}} lies in the Hiki region and identified Raidenyama Kofun as the tomb of Halfebi, the grandfather of {{ill|Owari no Omi|ja|乎獲居臣}}.<ref>Kuraji Nakamura, "The Musashi Provincial Family Conflict and Large-Scale Mounds," {{ill|Saitama Prefectural Museum of History and Folklore|ja|埼玉県立歴史と民俗の博物館}}.</ref>
== Excavated Artifacts == [[File:雷電山古墳 (東松山市) 出土 円筒埴輪.JPG|thumb|180px|right|{{center|Cylindrical haniwa}}{{small|Displayed at the {{ill|Saitama Prefectural Museum of History and Folklore|ja|埼玉県立歴史と民俗の博物館}}.}}]] Artifacts include {{ill|morning glory-shaped haniwa|ja|朝顔形埴輪}}, cylindrical haniwa, and stand-shaped haniwa. These artifacts differ significantly from commonly known haniwa, as they lack brush marks typically found on their surfaces. This indicates a manufacturing technique closer to {{ill|haji ware|ja|土師器}}. Evidence of open firing, resulting in blackened surfaces, suggests that these haniwa were made in the early 5th century. These artifacts are currently on display at the {{ill|Saitama Prefectural Museum of History and Folklore|ja|埼玉県立歴史と民俗の博物館}}.
== Notes == {{Reflist}}
== References == * Hiroshi Shiono, *Kofun of Saitama: Hiki and Chichibu*, pp. 67–75, Sakitama Publishing, 2004.
== See Also == * {{ill|List of kofun in Japan|ja|日本の古墳一覧}} * {{ill|List of kofun in Saitama Prefecture|ja|埼玉県の古墳一覧}}
== External Links == * [http://kofun.info/kofun/312 Raidenyama Kofun] - Kofun Map * [http://www.city.higashimatsuyama.lg.jp/kurashi/bunka_sports/bunka/bunkazai/shitei_bunkazai/1350459239838.html List of Cultural Properties] - Higashimatsuyama City

{{Infobox tumulus | name = Raidenyama Kofun | image = {{Image requested|cat=Higashimatsuyama}} | belonging_to = {{ill|Sanzenzuka Kofun Cluster|ja|三千塚古墳群}} | location = {{ill|Ōya, Higashimatsuyama|ja|東松山市大谷}}, {{ill|Higashimatsuyama|ja|東松山市}}, {{ill|Saitama Prefecture|ja|埼玉県}}, Japan | coordinates = {{Coord|36|05|13.8|N|139|23|17.8|E|region:JP|display=inline,title}} | shape = {{ill|Scallop-shaped tumulus|ja|帆立貝形古墳}} | size = Total length 85m, height 7m, rear circle diameter 73m, front width 39m | construction_era = Early 5th century | artifacts = {{ill|haniwa|ja|埴輪}} | historic_site_designation = Included in Sanzenzuka Kofun Cluster; designated as a municipal historic site on February 6, 1956<ref name="Higashimatsuyama">{{Cite web|url=http://www.city.higashimatsuyama.lg.jp/kurashi/bunka_sports/bunka/bunkazai/shitei_bunkazai/1350459239838.html|title=List of Cultural Properties|publisher=Higashimatsuyama City|date=2021-07-02|accessdate=2021-11-29}}</ref> | tangible_cultural_properties = Excavated haniwa, designated on March 26, 2004<ref name="Higashimatsuyama"/> | map = {{Location map|Japan Saitama|label=Raidenyama Kofun|lat_deg=36|lat_min=05|lat_sec=13.8|lon_deg=139|lon_min=23|lon_sec=17.8|mark=帆立貝形古墳-北西.svg|marksize=28|float=center|border=none|position=bottom|width=200|caption=Location in Saitama Prefecture}} }}
'''Raidenyama Kofun''' (雷電山古墳) is a {{ill|scallop-shaped tumulus|ja|帆立貝形古墳}} located in the {{ill|Sanzenzuka Kofun Cluster|ja|三千塚古墳群}} in {{ill|Higashimatsuyama|ja|東松山市}}, {{ill|Saitama Prefecture|ja|埼玉県}}. It is the oldest tumulus in Saitama Prefecture known to feature {{ill|haniwa|ja|埴輪}}.
== Overview == Constructed atop the 90-meter-high summit of Raidenyama Hill in Ōya, the tumulus was built with earth heaped only on the uppermost tier of the rear circle, while other parts were carved from the natural terrain. The tumulus shape is similar to the {{ill|Otomeyama Kofun|ja|乙女山古墳}} in Nara Prefecture. Excavation in 1984 revealed a three-tiered mound structure, with paving stones on the summit of the front section and four concentric rows of haniwa encircling the tumulus. These haniwa are the oldest found in Saitama Prefecture. A square arrangement of haniwa was also discovered on the rear summit, suggesting the presence of a burial facility beneath. The burial facility is presumed to be either a clay-enclosed burial chamber or a wooden coffin burial.
The site was designated as part of the "Sanzenzuka Kofun Cluster" municipal historic site in 1956, and the excavated haniwa were designated as municipal tangible cultural properties in 2004.
Isao Masuda hypothesized that the origin of the {{ill|Sakitama Kofun Cluster|ja|埼玉古墳群}} lies in the Hiki region and identified Raidenyama Kofun as the tomb of Halfebi, the grandfather of {{ill|Owari no Omi|ja|乎獲居臣}}.<ref>Kuraji Nakamura, "The Musashi Provincial Family Conflict and Large-Scale Mounds," {{ill|Saitama Prefectural Museum of History and Folklore|ja|埼玉県立歴史と民俗の博物館}}.</ref>
== Excavated Artifacts == [[File:雷電山古墳 (東松山市) 出土 円筒埴輪.JPG|thumb|180px|right|{{center|Cylindrical haniwa}}{{small|Displayed at the {{ill|Saitama Prefectural Museum of History and Folklore|ja|埼玉県立歴史と民俗の博物館}}.}}]] Artifacts include {{ill|morning glory-shaped haniwa|ja|朝顔形埴輪}}, cylindrical haniwa, and stand-shaped haniwa. These artifacts differ significantly from commonly known haniwa, as they lack brush marks typically found on their surfaces. This indicates a manufacturing technique closer to {{ill|haji ware|ja|土師器}}. Evidence of open firing, resulting in blackened surfaces, suggests that these haniwa were made in the early 5th century. These artifacts are currently on display at the {{ill|Saitama Prefectural Museum of History and Folklore|ja|埼玉県立歴史と民俗の博物館}}.
== Notes == {{Reflist}}
== References == * Hiroshi Shiono, *Kofun of Saitama: Hiki and Chichibu*, pp. 67–75, Sakitama Publishing, 2004.
== See Also == * {{ill|List of kofun in Japan|ja|日本の古墳一覧}} * {{ill|List of kofun in Saitama Prefecture|ja|埼玉県の古墳一覧}}
== External Links == * [http://kofun.info/kofun/312 Raidenyama Kofun] - Kofun Map * [http://www.city.higashimatsuyama.lg.jp/kurashi/bunka_sports/bunka/bunkazai/shitei_bunkazai/1350459239838.html List of Cultural Properties] - Higashimatsuyama City

These haniwa are the oldest found in Saitama Prefecture. A square arrangement of haniwa was also discovered on the rear summit, suggesting the presence of a burial facility beneath. The burial facility is presumed to be either a clay-enclosed burial chamber or a wooden coffin burial.
The site was designated as part of the "Sanzenzuka Kofun Cluster" municipal historic site in 1956, and the excavated haniwa were designated as municipal tangible cultural properties in 2004.
Isao Masuda hypothesized that the origin of the {{ill|Sakitama Kofun Cluster|ja|埼玉古墳群}} lies in the Hiki region and identified Raidenyama Kofun as the tomb of Halfebi, the grandfather of {{ill|Owari no Omi|ja|乎獲居臣}}.<ref>Kuraji Nakamura, "The Musashi Provincial Family Conflict and Large-Scale Mounds," {{ill|Saitama Prefectural Museum of History and Folklore|ja|埼玉県立歴史と民俗の博物館}}.</ref>
== Excavated Artifacts == [[File:雷電山古墳 (東松山市) 出土 円筒埴輪.JPG|thumb|180px|right|{{center|Cylindrical haniwa}}{{small|Displayed at the {{ill|Saitama Prefectural Museum of History and Folklore|ja|埼玉県立歴史と民俗の博物館}}.}}]] Artifacts include {{ill|morning glory-shaped haniwa|ja|朝顔形埴輪}}, cylindrical haniwa, and stand-shaped haniwa. These artifacts differ significantly from commonly known haniwa, as they lack brush marks typically found on their surfaces. This indicates a manufacturing technique closer to {{ill|haji ware|ja|土師器}}. Evidence of open firing, resulting in blackened surfaces, suggests that these haniwa were made in the early 5th century. These artifacts are currently on display at the {{ill|Saitama Prefectural Museum of History and Folklore|ja|埼玉県立歴史と民俗の博物館}}.

== Overview == Constructed atop the 90-meter-high summit of Raidenyama Hill in Ōya, the tumulus was built with earth heaped only on the uppermost tier of the rear circle, while other parts were carved from the natural terrain. The tumulus shape is similar to the {{ill|Otomeyama Kofun|ja|乙女山古墳}} in Nara Prefecture. Excavation in 1984 revealed a three-tiered mound structure, with paving stones on the summit of the front section and four concentric rows of haniwa encircling the tumulus. These haniwa are the oldest found in Saitama Prefecture. A square arrangement of haniwa was also discovered on the rear summit, suggesting the presence of a burial facility beneath. The burial facility is presumed to be either a clay-enclosed burial chamber or a wooden coffin burial.
The site was designated as part of the "Sanzenzuka Kofun Cluster" municipal historic site in 1956, and the excavated haniwa were designated as municipal tangible cultural properties in 2004.
Isao Masuda hypothesized that the origin of the {{ill|Sakitama Kofun Cluster|ja|埼玉古墳群}} lies in the Hiki region and identified Raidenyama Kofun as the tomb of Halfebi, the grandfather of {{ill|Owari no Omi|ja|乎獲居臣}}.<ref>Kuraji Nakamura, "The Musashi Provincial Family Conflict and Large-Scale Mounds," {{ill|Saitama Prefectural Museum of History and Folklore|ja|埼玉県立歴史と民俗の博物館}}.</ref>

These artifacts differ significantly from commonly known haniwa, as they lack brush marks typically found on their surfaces. This indicates a manufacturing technique closer to {{ill|haji ware|ja|土師器}}. Evidence of open firing, resulting in blackened surfaces, suggests that these haniwa were made in the early 5th century. These artifacts are currently on display at the {{ill|Saitama Prefectural Museum of History and Folklore|ja|埼玉県立歴史と民俗の博物館}}.

== Excavated Artifacts == [[File:雷電山古墳 (東松山市) 出土 円筒埴輪.JPG|thumb|180px|right|{{center|Cylindrical haniwa}}{{small|Displayed at the {{ill|Saitama Prefectural Museum of History and Folklore|ja|埼玉県立歴史と民俗の博物館}}.}}]] Artifacts include {{ill|morning glory-shaped haniwa|ja|朝顔形埴輪}}, cylindrical haniwa, and stand-shaped haniwa. These artifacts differ significantly from commonly known haniwa, as they lack brush marks typically found on their surfaces. This indicates a manufacturing technique closer to {{ill|haji ware|ja|土師器}}. Evidence of open firing, resulting in blackened surfaces, suggests that these haniwa were made in the early 5th century. These artifacts are currently on display at the {{ill|Saitama Prefectural Museum of History and Folklore|ja|埼玉県立歴史と民俗の博物館}}.

'''Raidenyama Kofun''' (雷電山古墳) is a {{ill|scallop-shaped tumulus|ja|帆立貝形古墳}} located in the {{ill|Sanzenzuka Kofun Cluster|ja|三千塚古墳群}} in {{ill|Higashimatsuyama|ja|東松山市}}, {{ill|Saitama Prefecture|ja|埼玉県}}. It is the oldest tumulus in Saitama Prefecture known to feature {{ill|haniwa|ja|埴輪}}.
== Overview == Constructed atop the 90-meter-high summit of Raidenyama Hill in Ōya, the tumulus was built with earth heaped only on the uppermost tier of the rear circle, while other parts were carved from the natural terrain. The tumulus shape is similar to the {{ill|Otomeyama Kofun|ja|乙女山古墳}} in Nara Prefecture. Excavation in 1984 revealed a three-tiered mound structure, with paving stones on the summit of the front section and four concentric rows of haniwa encircling the tumulus. These haniwa are the oldest found in Saitama Prefecture. A square arrangement of haniwa was also discovered on the rear summit, suggesting the presence of a burial facility beneath. The burial facility is presumed to be either a clay-enclosed burial chamber or a wooden coffin burial.
The site was designated as part of the "Sanzenzuka Kofun Cluster" municipal historic site in 1956, and the excavated haniwa were designated as municipal tangible cultural properties in 2004.
Isao Masuda hypothesized that the origin of the {{ill|Sakitama Kofun Cluster|ja|埼玉古墳群}} lies in the Hiki region and identified Raidenyama Kofun as the tomb of Halfebi, the grandfather of {{ill|Owari no Omi|ja|乎獲居臣}}.<ref>Kuraji Nakamura, "The Musashi Provincial Family Conflict and Large-Scale Mounds," {{ill|Saitama Prefectural Museum of History and Folklore|ja|埼玉県立歴史と民俗の博物館}}.</ref>
== Excavated Artifacts == [[File:雷電山古墳 (東松山市) 出土 円筒埴輪.JPG|thumb|180px|right|{{center|Cylindrical haniwa}}{{small|Displayed at the {{ill|Saitama Prefectural Museum of History and Folklore|ja|埼玉県立歴史と民俗の博物館}}.}}]] Artifacts include {{ill|morning glory-shaped haniwa|ja|朝顔形埴輪}}, cylindrical haniwa, and stand-shaped haniwa. These artifacts differ significantly from commonly known haniwa, as they lack brush marks typically found on their surfaces. This indicates a manufacturing technique closer to {{ill|haji ware|ja|土師器}}. Evidence of open firing, resulting in blackened surfaces, suggests that these haniwa were made in the early 5th century. These artifacts are currently on display at the {{ill|Saitama Prefectural Museum of History and Folklore|ja|埼玉県立歴史と民俗の博物館}}.

== References == * Hiroshi Shiono, *Kofun of Saitama: Hiki and Chichibu*, pp. 67–75, Sakitama Publishing, 2004.

== Excavated Artifacts == [[File:雷電山古墳 (東松山市) 出土 円筒埴輪.JPG|thumb|180px|right|{{center|Cylindrical haniwa}}{{small|Displayed at the {{ill|Saitama Prefectural Museum of History and Folklore|ja|埼玉県立歴史と民俗の博物館}}.}}]] Artifacts include {{ill|morning glory-shaped haniwa|ja|朝顔形埴輪}}, cylindrical haniwa, and stand-shaped haniwa. These artifacts differ significantly from commonly known haniwa, as they lack brush marks typically found on their surfaces. This indicates a manufacturing technique closer to {{ill|haji ware|ja|土師器}}. Evidence of open firing, resulting in blackened surfaces, suggests that these haniwa were made in the early 5th century. These artifacts are currently on display at the {{ill|Saitama Prefectural Museum of History and Folklore|ja|埼玉県立歴史と民俗の博物館}}.
== Notes == {{Reflist}}

The tumulus shape is similar to the {{ill|Otomeyama Kofun|ja|乙女山古墳}} in Nara Prefecture. Excavation in 1984 revealed a three-tiered mound structure, with paving stones on the summit of the front section and four concentric rows of haniwa encircling the tumulus. These haniwa are the oldest found in Saitama Prefecture. A square arrangement of haniwa was also discovered on the rear summit, suggesting the presence of a burial facility beneath. The burial facility is presumed to be either a clay-enclosed burial chamber or a wooden coffin burial.
The site was designated as part of the "Sanzenzuka Kofun Cluster" municipal historic site in 1956, and the excavated haniwa were designated as municipal tangible cultural properties in 2004.
Isao Masuda hypothesized that the origin of the {{ill|Sakitama Kofun Cluster|ja|埼玉古墳群}} lies in the Hiki region and identified Raidenyama Kofun as the tomb of Halfebi, the grandfather of {{ill|Owari no Omi|ja|乎獲居臣}}.<ref>Kuraji Nakamura, "The Musashi Provincial Family Conflict and Large-Scale Mounds," {{ill|Saitama Prefectural Museum of History and Folklore|ja|埼玉県立歴史と民俗の博物館}}.</ref>
== Excavated Artifacts == [[File:雷電山古墳 (東松山市) 出土 円筒埴輪.JPG|thumb|180px|right|{{center|Cylindrical haniwa}}{{small|Displayed at the {{ill|Saitama Prefectural Museum of History and Folklore|ja|埼玉県立歴史と民俗の博物館}}.}}]] Artifacts include {{ill|morning glory-shaped haniwa|ja|朝顔形埴輪}}, cylindrical haniwa, and stand-shaped haniwa. These artifacts differ significantly from commonly known haniwa, as they lack brush marks typically found on their surfaces. This indicates a manufacturing technique closer to {{ill|haji ware|ja|土師器}}. Evidence of open firing, resulting in blackened surfaces, suggests that these haniwa were made in the early 5th century. These artifacts are currently on display at the {{ill|Saitama Prefectural Museum of History and Folklore|ja|埼玉県立歴史と民俗の博物館}}.
== Notes == {{Reflist}}
== References == * Hiroshi Shiono, *Kofun of Saitama: Hiki and Chichibu*, pp. 67–75, Sakitama Publishing, 2004.
== See Also == * {{ill|List of kofun in Japan|ja|日本の古墳一覧}} * {{ill|List of kofun in Saitama Prefecture|ja|埼玉県の古墳一覧}}

{{Infobox tumulus | name = Raidenyama Kofun | image = {{Image requested|cat=Higashimatsuyama}} | belonging_to = {{ill|Sanzenzuka Kofun Cluster|ja|三千塚古墳群}} | location = {{ill|Ōya, Higashimatsuyama|ja|東松山市大谷}}, {{ill|Higashimatsuyama|ja|東松山市}}, {{ill|Saitama Prefecture|ja|埼玉県}}, Japan | coordinates = {{Coord|36|05|13.8|N|139|23|17.8|E|region:JP|display=inline,title}} | shape = {{ill|Scallop-shaped tumulus|ja|帆立貝形古墳}} | size = Total length 85m, height 7m, rear circle diameter 73m, front width 39m | construction_era = Early 5th century | artifacts = {{ill|haniwa|ja|埴輪}} | historic_site_designation = Included in Sanzenzuka Kofun Cluster; designated as a municipal historic site on February 6, 1956<ref name="Higashimatsuyama">{{Cite web|url=http://www.city.higashimatsuyama.lg.jp/kurashi/bunka_sports/bunka/bunkazai/shitei_bunkazai/1350459239838.html|title=List of Cultural Properties|publisher=Higashimatsuyama City|date=2021-07-02|accessdate=2021-11-29}}</ref> | tangible_cultural_properties = Excavated haniwa, designated on March 26, 2004<ref name="Higashimatsuyama"/> | map = {{Location map|Japan Saitama|label=Raidenyama Kofun|lat_deg=36|lat_min=05|lat_sec=13.8|lon_deg=139|lon_min=23|lon_sec=17.8|mark=帆立貝形古墳-北西.svg|marksize=28|float=center|border=none|position=bottom|width=200|caption=Location in Saitama Prefecture}} }}
'''Raidenyama Kofun''' (雷電山古墳) is a {{ill|scallop-shaped tumulus|ja|帆立貝形古墳}} located in the {{ill|Sanzenzuka Kofun Cluster|ja|三千塚古墳群}} in {{ill|Higashimatsuyama|ja|東松山市}}, {{ill|Saitama Prefecture|ja|埼玉県}}. It is the oldest tumulus in Saitama Prefecture known to feature {{ill|haniwa|ja|埴輪}}.
== Overview == Constructed atop the 90-meter-high summit of Raidenyama Hill in Ōya, the tumulus was built with earth heaped only on the uppermost tier of the rear circle, while other parts were carved from the natural terrain. The tumulus shape is similar to the {{ill|Otomeyama Kofun|ja|乙女山古墳}} in Nara Prefecture. Excavation in 1984 revealed a three-tiered mound structure, with paving stones on the summit of the front section and four concentric rows of haniwa encircling the tumulus. These haniwa are the oldest found in Saitama Prefecture. A square arrangement of haniwa was also discovered on the rear summit, suggesting the presence of a burial facility beneath. The burial facility is presumed to be either a clay-enclosed burial chamber or a wooden coffin burial.
The site was designated as part of the "Sanzenzuka Kofun Cluster" municipal historic site in 1956, and the excavated haniwa were designated as municipal tangible cultural properties in 2004.
Isao Masuda hypothesized that the origin of the {{ill|Sakitama Kofun Cluster|ja|埼玉古墳群}} lies in the Hiki region and identified Raidenyama Kofun as the tomb of Halfebi, the grandfather of {{ill|Owari no Omi|ja|乎獲居臣}}.<ref>Kuraji Nakamura, "The Musashi Provincial Family Conflict and Large-Scale Mounds," {{ill|Saitama Prefectural Museum of History and Folklore|ja|埼玉県立歴史と民俗の博物館}}.</ref>
== Excavated Artifacts == [[File:雷電山古墳 (東松山市) 出土 円筒埴輪.JPG|thumb|180px|right|{{center|Cylindrical haniwa}}{{small|Displayed at the {{ill|Saitama Prefectural Museum of History and Folklore|ja|埼玉県立歴史と民俗の博物館}}.}}]] Artifacts include {{ill|morning glory-shaped haniwa|ja|朝顔形埴輪}}, cylindrical haniwa, and stand-shaped haniwa. These artifacts differ significantly from commonly known haniwa, as they lack brush marks typically found on their surfaces. This indicates a manufacturing technique closer to {{ill|haji ware|ja|土師器}}. Evidence of open firing, resulting in blackened surfaces, suggests that these haniwa were made in the early 5th century. These artifacts are currently on display at the {{ill|Saitama Prefectural Museum of History and Folklore|ja|埼玉県立歴史と民俗の博物館}}.
== Notes == {{Reflist}}
== References == * Hiroshi Shiono, *Kofun of Saitama: Hiki and Chichibu*, pp. 67–75, Sakitama Publishing, 2004.
== See Also == * {{ill|List of kofun in Japan|ja|日本の古墳一覧}} * {{ill|List of kofun in Saitama Prefecture|ja|埼玉県の古墳一覧}}
== External Links == * [http://kofun.info/kofun/312 Raidenyama Kofun] - Kofun Map * [http://www.city.higashimatsuyama.lg.jp/kurashi/bunka_sports/bunka/bunkazai/shitei_bunkazai/1350459239838.html List of Cultural Properties] - Higashimatsuyama City

{{Infobox tumulus | name = Raidenyama Kofun | image = {{Image requested|cat=Higashimatsuyama}} | belonging_to = {{ill|Sanzenzuka Kofun Cluster|ja|三千塚古墳群}} | location = {{ill|Ōya, Higashimatsuyama|ja|東松山市大谷}}, {{ill|Higashimatsuyama|ja|東松山市}}, {{ill|Saitama Prefecture|ja|埼玉県}}, Japan | coordinates = {{Coord|36|05|13.8|N|139|23|17.8|E|region:JP|display=inline,title}} | shape = {{ill|Scallop-shaped tumulus|ja|帆立貝形古墳}} | size = Total length 85m, height 7m, rear circle diameter 73m, front width 39m | construction_era = Early 5th century | artifacts = {{ill|haniwa|ja|埴輪}} | historic_site_designation = Included in Sanzenzuka Kofun Cluster; designated as a municipal historic site on February 6, 1956<ref name="Higashimatsuyama">{{Cite web|url=http://www.city.higashimatsuyama.lg.jp/kurashi/bunka_sports/bunka/bunkazai/shitei_bunkazai/1350459239838.html|title=List of Cultural Properties|publisher=Higashimatsuyama City|date=2021-07-02|accessdate=2021-11-29}}</ref> | tangible_cultural_properties = Excavated haniwa, designated on March 26, 2004<ref name="Higashimatsuyama"/> | map = {{Location map|Japan Saitama|label=Raidenyama Kofun|lat_deg=36|lat_min=05|lat_sec=13.8|lon_deg=139|lon_min=23|lon_sec=17.8|mark=帆立貝形古墳-北西.svg|marksize=28|float=center|border=none|position=bottom|width=200|caption=Location in Saitama Prefecture}} }}
'''Raidenyama Kofun''' (雷電山古墳) is a {{ill|scallop-shaped tumulus|ja|帆立貝形古墳}} located in the {{ill|Sanzenzuka Kofun Cluster|ja|三千塚古墳群}} in {{ill|Higashimatsuyama|ja|東松山市}}, {{ill|Saitama Prefecture|ja|埼玉県}}. It is the oldest tumulus in Saitama Prefecture known to feature {{ill|haniwa|ja|埴輪}}.
== Overview == Constructed atop the 90-meter-high summit of Raidenyama Hill in Ōya, the tumulus was built with earth heaped only on the uppermost tier of the rear circle, while other parts were carved from the natural terrain. The tumulus shape is similar to the {{ill|Otomeyama Kofun|ja|乙女山古墳}} in Nara Prefecture. Excavation in 1984 revealed a three-tiered mound structure, with paving stones on the summit of the front section and four concentric rows of haniwa encircling the tumulus. These haniwa are the oldest found in Saitama Prefecture. A square arrangement of haniwa was also discovered on the rear summit, suggesting the presence of a burial facility beneath. The burial facility is presumed to be either a clay-enclosed burial chamber or a wooden coffin burial.
The site was designated as part of the "Sanzenzuka Kofun Cluster" municipal historic site in 1956, and the excavated haniwa were designated as municipal tangible cultural properties in 2004.
Isao Masuda hypothesized that the origin of the {{ill|Sakitama Kofun Cluster|ja|埼玉古墳群}} lies in the Hiki region and identified Raidenyama Kofun as the tomb of Halfebi, the grandfather of {{ill|Owari no Omi|ja|乎獲居臣}}.<ref>Kuraji Nakamura, "The Musashi Provincial Family Conflict and Large-Scale Mounds," {{ill|Saitama Prefectural Museum of History and Folklore|ja|埼玉県立歴史と民俗の博物館}}.</ref>
== Excavated Artifacts == [[File:雷電山古墳 (東松山市) 出土 円筒埴輪.JPG|thumb|180px|right|{{center|Cylindrical haniwa}}{{small|Displayed at the {{ill|Saitama Prefectural Museum of History and Folklore|ja|埼玉県立歴史と民俗の博物館}}.}}]] Artifacts include {{ill|morning glory-shaped haniwa|ja|朝顔形埴輪}}, cylindrical haniwa, and stand-shaped haniwa. These artifacts differ significantly from commonly known haniwa, as they lack brush marks typically found on their surfaces. This indicates a manufacturing technique closer to {{ill|haji ware|ja|土師器}}. Evidence of open firing, resulting in blackened surfaces, suggests that these haniwa were made in the early 5th century. These artifacts are currently on display at the {{ill|Saitama Prefectural Museum of History and Folklore|ja|埼玉県立歴史と民俗の博物館}}.
== Notes == {{Reflist}}
== References == * Hiroshi Shiono, *Kofun of Saitama: Hiki and Chichibu*, pp. 67–75, Sakitama Publishing, 2004.
== See Also == * {{ill|List of kofun in Japan|ja|日本の古墳一覧}} * {{ill|List of kofun in Saitama Prefecture|ja|埼玉県の古墳一覧}}
== External Links == * [http://kofun.info/kofun/312 Raidenyama Kofun] - Kofun Map * [http://www.city.higashimatsuyama.lg.jp/kurashi/bunka_sports/bunka/bunkazai/shitei_bunkazai/1350459239838.html List of Cultural Properties] - Higashimatsuyama City

'''Raidenyama Kofun''' (雷電山古墳) is a {{ill|scallop-shaped tumulus|ja|帆立貝形古墳}} located in the {{ill|Sanzenzuka Kofun Cluster|ja|三千塚古墳群}} in {{ill|Higashimatsuyama|ja|東松山市}}, {{ill|Saitama Prefecture|ja|埼玉県}}. It is the oldest tumulus in Saitama Prefecture known to feature {{ill|haniwa|ja|埴輪}}.
== Overview == Constructed atop the 90-meter-high summit of Raidenyama Hill in Ōya, the tumulus was built with earth heaped only on the uppermost tier of the rear circle, while other parts were carved from the natural terrain. The tumulus shape is similar to the {{ill|Otomeyama Kofun|ja|乙女山古墳}} in Nara Prefecture. Excavation in 1984 revealed a three-tiered mound structure, with paving stones on the summit of the front section and four concentric rows of haniwa encircling the tumulus. These haniwa are the oldest found in Saitama Prefecture. A square arrangement of haniwa was also discovered on the rear summit, suggesting the presence of a burial facility beneath. The burial facility is presumed to be either a clay-enclosed burial chamber or a wooden coffin burial.
The site was designated as part of the "Sanzenzuka Kofun Cluster" municipal historic site in 1956, and the excavated haniwa were designated as municipal tangible cultural properties in 2004.
Isao Masuda hypothesized that the origin of the {{ill|Sakitama Kofun Cluster|ja|埼玉古墳群}} lies in the Hiki region and identified Raidenyama Kofun as the tomb of Halfebi, the grandfather of {{ill|Owari no Omi|ja|乎獲居臣}}.<ref>Kuraji Nakamura, "The Musashi Provincial Family Conflict and Large-Scale Mounds," {{ill|Saitama Prefectural Museum of History and Folklore|ja|埼玉県立歴史と民俗の博物館}}.</ref>
== Excavated Artifacts == [[File:雷電山古墳 (東松山市) 出土 円筒埴輪.JPG|thumb|180px|right|{{center|Cylindrical haniwa}}{{small|Displayed at the {{ill|Saitama Prefectural Museum of History and Folklore|ja|埼玉県立歴史と民俗の博物館}}.}}]] Artifacts include {{ill|morning glory-shaped haniwa|ja|朝顔形埴輪}}, cylindrical haniwa, and stand-shaped haniwa. These artifacts differ significantly from commonly known haniwa, as they lack brush marks typically found on their surfaces. This indicates a manufacturing technique closer to {{ill|haji ware|ja|土師器}}. Evidence of open firing, resulting in blackened surfaces, suggests that these haniwa were made in the early 5th century. These artifacts are currently on display at the {{ill|Saitama Prefectural Museum of History and Folklore|ja|埼玉県立歴史と民俗の博物館}}.
== Notes == {{Reflist}}
== References == * Hiroshi Shiono, *Kofun of Saitama: Hiki and Chichibu*, pp. 67–75, Sakitama Publishing, 2004.
== See Also == * {{ill|List of kofun in Japan|ja|日本の古墳一覧}} * {{ill|List of kofun in Saitama Prefecture|ja|埼玉県の古墳一覧}}
== External Links == * [http://kofun.info/kofun/312 Raidenyama Kofun] - Kofun Map * [http://www.city.higashimatsuyama.lg.jp/kurashi/bunka_sports/bunka/bunkazai/shitei_bunkazai/1350459239838.html List of Cultural Properties] - Higashimatsuyama City

revision.text:
{{Infobox tumulus | name = Raidenyama Kofun | image = {{Image requested|cat=Higashimatsuyama}} | belonging_to = {{ill|Sanzenzuka Kofun Cluster|ja|三千塚古墳群}} | location = {{ill|Ōya, Higashimatsuyama|ja|東松山市大谷}}, {{ill|Higashimatsuyama|ja|東松山市}}, {{ill|Saitama Prefecture|ja|埼玉県}}, Japan | coordinates = {{Coord|36|05|13.8|N|139|23|17.8|E|region:JP|display=inline,title}} | shape = {{ill|Scallop-shaped tumulus|ja|帆立貝形古墳|1=Scallop Kofun}} | size = Total length 85m, height 7m, rear circle diameter 73m, front width 39m | construction_era = Early 5th century | artifacts = {{ill|haniwa|ja|埴輪}} | historic_site_designation = Included in Sanzenzuka Kofun Cluster; designated as a municipal historic site on February 6, 1956<ref name="Higashimatsuyama">{{Cite web|url=http://www.city.higashimatsuyama.lg.jp/kurashi/bunka_sports/bunka/bunkazai/shitei_bunkazai/1350459239838.html|title=List of Cultural Properties|publisher=Higashimatsuyama City|date=2021-07-02|accessdate=2021-11-29}}</ref> | tangible_cultural_properties = Excavated haniwa, designated on March 26, 2004<ref name="Higashimatsuyama"/> | map = {{Location map|Japan Saitama|label=Raidenyama Kofun|lat_deg=36|lat_min=05|lat_sec=13.8|lon_deg=139|lon_min=23|lon_sec=17.8|mark=帆立貝形古墳-北西.svg|marksize=28|float=center|border=none|position=bottom|width=200|caption=Location in Saitama Prefecture}} }}
'''Raidenyama Kofun''' (雷電山古墳) is a {{ill|scallop-shaped tumulus|ja|帆立貝形古墳|1=Scallop Kofun}} located in the {{ill|Sanzenzuka Kofun Cluster|ja|三千塚古墳群}} in {{ill|Higashimatsuyama|ja|東松山市}}, {{ill|Saitama Prefecture|ja|埼玉県}}. It is the oldest tumulus in Saitama Prefecture known to feature {{ill|haniwa|ja|埴輪}}.
== Overview == Constructed atop the 90-meter-high summit of Raidenyama Hill in Ōya, the tumulus was built with earth heaped only on the uppermost tier of the rear circle, while other parts were carved from the natural terrain. The tumulus shape is similar to the {{ill|Otomeyama Kofun|ja|乙女山古墳}} in Nara Prefecture. Excavation in 1984 revealed a three-tiered mound structure, with paving stones on the summit of the front section and four concentric rows of haniwa encircling the tumulus. These haniwa are the oldest found in Saitama Prefecture. A square arrangement of haniwa was also discovered on the rear summit, suggesting the presence of a burial facility beneath. The burial facility is presumed to be either a clay-enclosed burial chamber or a wooden coffin burial.
The site was designated as part of the "Sanzenzuka Kofun Cluster" municipal historic site in 1956, and the excavated haniwa were designated as municipal tangible cultural properties in 2004.
Isao Masuda hypothesized that the origin of the {{ill|Sakitama Kofun Cluster|ja|埼玉古墳群}} lies in the Hiki region and identified Raidenyama Kofun as the tomb of Halfebi, the grandfather of {{ill|Owari no Omi|ja|乎獲居臣}}.<ref>Kuraji Nakamura, "The Musashi Provincial Family Conflict and Large-Scale Mounds," {{ill|Saitama Prefectural Museum of History and Folklore|ja|埼玉県立歴史と民俗の博物館}}.</ref>
== Excavated Artifacts == [[File:雷電山古墳 (東松山市) 出土 円筒埴輪.JPG|thumb|180px|right|{{center|Cylindrical haniwa}}{{small|Displayed at the {{ill|Saitama Prefectural Museum of History and Folklore|ja|埼玉県立歴史と民俗の博物館}}.}}]] Artifacts include {{ill|morning glory-shaped haniwa|ja|朝顔形埴輪|1=Morning glory-shaped haniwa}}, cylindrical haniwa, and stand-shaped haniwa. These artifacts differ significantly from commonly known haniwa, as they lack brush marks typically found on their surfaces. This indicates a manufacturing technique closer to {{ill|haji ware|ja|土師器}}. Evidence of open firing, resulting in blackened surfaces, suggests that these haniwa were made in the early 5th century. These artifacts are currently on display at the {{ill|Saitama Prefectural Museum of History and Folklore|ja|埼玉県立歴史と民俗の博物館}}.
== Notes == {{Reflist}}
== References == * Hiroshi Shiono, *Kofun of Saitama: Hiki and Chichibu*, pp. 67–75, Sakitama Publishing, 2004.
== See Also == * {{ill|List of kofun in Japan|ja|日本の古墳一覧}} * {{ill|List of kofun in Saitama Prefecture|ja|埼玉県の古墳一覧}}
== External Links == * [http://kofun.info/kofun/312 Raidenyama Kofun] - Kofun Map * [http://www.city.higashimatsuyama.lg.jp/kurashi/bunka_sports/bunka/bunkazai/shitei_bunkazai/1350459239838.html List of Cultural Properties] - Higashimatsuyama City

{{Infobox tumulus | name = Raidenyama Kofun | image = {{Image requested|cat=Higashimatsuyama}} | belonging_to = {{ill|Sanzenzuka Kofun Cluster|ja|三千塚古墳群}} | location = {{ill|Ōya, Higashimatsuyama|ja|東松山市大谷}}, {{ill|Higashimatsuyama|ja|東松山市}}, {{ill|Saitama Prefecture|ja|埼玉県}}, Japan | coordinates = {{Coord|36|05|13.8|N|139|23|17.8|E|region:JP|display=inline,title}} | shape = {{ill|Scallop-shaped tumulus|ja|帆立貝形古墳|1=Scallop Kofun}} | size = Total length 85m, height 7m, rear circle diameter 73m, front width 39m | construction_era = Early 5th century | artifacts = {{ill|haniwa|ja|埴輪}} | historic_site_designation = Included in Sanzenzuka Kofun Cluster; designated as a municipal historic site on February 6, 1956<ref name="Higashimatsuyama">{{Cite web|url=http://www.city.higashimatsuyama.lg.jp/kurashi/bunka_sports/bunka/bunkazai/shitei_bunkazai/1350459239838.html|title=List of Cultural Properties|publisher=Higashimatsuyama City|date=2021-07-02|accessdate=2021-11-29}}</ref> | tangible_cultural_properties = Excavated haniwa, designated on March 26, 2004<ref name="Higashimatsuyama"/> | map = {{Location map|Japan Saitama|label=Raidenyama Kofun|lat_deg=36|lat_min=05|lat_sec=13.8|lon_deg=139|lon_min=23|lon_sec=17.8|mark=帆立貝形古墳-北西.svg|marksize=28|float=center|border=none|position=bottom|width=200|caption=Location in Saitama Prefecture}} }}
'''Raidenyama Kofun''' (雷電山古墳) is a {{ill|scallop-shaped tumulus|ja|帆立貝形古墳|1=Scallop Kofun}} located in the {{ill|Sanzenzuka Kofun Cluster|ja|三千塚古墳群}} in {{ill|Higashimatsuyama|ja|東松山市}}, {{ill|Saitama Prefecture|ja|埼玉県}}. It is the oldest tumulus in Saitama Prefecture known to feature {{ill|haniwa|ja|埴輪}}.
== Overview == Constructed atop the 90-meter-high summit of Raidenyama Hill in Ōya, the tumulus was built with earth heaped only on the uppermost tier of the rear circle, while other parts were carved from the natural terrain. The tumulus shape is similar to the {{ill|Otomeyama Kofun|ja|乙女山古墳}} in Nara Prefecture. Excavation in 1984 revealed a three-tiered mound structure, with paving stones on the summit of the front section and four concentric rows of haniwa encircling the tumulus. These haniwa are the oldest found in Saitama Prefecture. A square arrangement of haniwa was also discovered on the rear summit, suggesting the presence of a burial facility beneath. The burial facility is presumed to be either a clay-enclosed burial chamber or a wooden coffin burial.
The site was designated as part of the "Sanzenzuka Kofun Cluster" municipal historic site in 1956, and the excavated haniwa were designated as municipal tangible cultural properties in 2004.
Isao Masuda hypothesized that the origin of the {{ill|Sakitama Kofun Cluster|ja|埼玉古墳群}} lies in the Hiki region and identified Raidenyama Kofun as the tomb of Halfebi, the grandfather of {{ill|Owari no Omi|ja|乎獲居臣}}.<ref>Kuraji Nakamura, "The Musashi Provincial Family Conflict and Large-Scale Mounds," {{ill|Saitama Prefectural Museum of History and Folklore|ja|埼玉県立歴史と民俗の博物館}}.</ref>
== Excavated Artifacts == [[File:雷電山古墳 (東松山市) 出土 円筒埴輪.JPG|thumb|180px|right|{{center|Cylindrical haniwa}}{{small|Displayed at the {{ill|Saitama Prefectural Museum of History and Folklore|ja|埼玉県立歴史と民俗の博物館}}.}}]] Artifacts include {{ill|morning glory-shaped haniwa|ja|朝顔形埴輪|1=Morning glory-shaped haniwa}}, cylindrical haniwa, and stand-shaped haniwa. These artifacts differ significantly from commonly known haniwa, as they lack brush marks typically found on their surfaces. This indicates a manufacturing technique closer to {{ill|haji ware|ja|土師器}}. Evidence of open firing, resulting in blackened surfaces, suggests that these haniwa were made in the early 5th century. These artifacts are currently on display at the {{ill|Saitama Prefectural Museum of History and Folklore|ja|埼玉県立歴史と民俗の博物館}}.
== Notes == {{Reflist}}
== References == * Hiroshi Shiono, *Kofun of Saitama: Hiki and Chichibu*, pp. 67–75, Sakitama Publishing, 2004.
== See Also == * {{ill|List of kofun in Japan|ja|日本の古墳一覧}} * {{ill|List of kofun in Saitama Prefecture|ja|埼玉県の古墳一覧}}
== External Links == * [http://kofun.info/kofun/312 Raidenyama Kofun] - Kofun Map * [http://www.city.higashimatsuyama.lg.jp/kurashi/bunka_sports/bunka/bunkazai/shitei_bunkazai/1350459239838.html List of Cultural Properties] - Higashimatsuyama City

== Excavated Artifacts == [[File:雷電山古墳 (東松山市) 出土 円筒埴輪.JPG|thumb|180px|right|{{center|Cylindrical haniwa}}{{small|Displayed at the {{ill|Saitama Prefectural Museum of History and Folklore|ja|埼玉県立歴史と民俗の博物館}}.}}]] Artifacts include {{ill|morning glory-shaped haniwa|ja|朝顔形埴輪|1=Morning glory-shaped haniwa}}, cylindrical haniwa, and stand-shaped haniwa. These artifacts differ significantly from commonly known haniwa, as they lack brush marks typically found on their surfaces. This indicates a manufacturing technique closer to {{ill|haji ware|ja|土師器}}. Evidence of open firing, resulting in blackened surfaces, suggests that these haniwa were made in the early 5th century. These artifacts are currently on display at the {{ill|Saitama Prefectural Museum of History and Folklore|ja|埼玉県立歴史と民俗の博物館}}.

== References == * Hiroshi Shiono, *Kofun of Saitama: Hiki and Chichibu*, pp. 67–75, Sakitama Publishing, 2004.

It is the oldest tumulus in Saitama Prefecture known to feature {{ill|haniwa|ja|埴輪}}.
== Overview == Constructed atop the 90-meter-high summit of Raidenyama Hill in Ōya, the tumulus was built with earth heaped only on the uppermost tier of the rear circle, while other parts were carved from the natural terrain. The tumulus shape is similar to the {{ill|Otomeyama Kofun|ja|乙女山古墳}} in Nara Prefecture. Excavation in 1984 revealed a three-tiered mound structure, with paving stones on the summit of the front section and four concentric rows of haniwa encircling the tumulus. These haniwa are the oldest found in Saitama Prefecture. A square arrangement of haniwa was also discovered on the rear summit, suggesting the presence of a burial facility beneath. The burial facility is presumed to be either a clay-enclosed burial chamber or a wooden coffin burial.
The site was designated as part of the "Sanzenzuka Kofun Cluster" municipal historic site in 1956, and the excavated haniwa were designated as municipal tangible cultural properties in 2004.
Isao Masuda hypothesized that the origin of the {{ill|Sakitama Kofun Cluster|ja|埼玉古墳群}} lies in the Hiki region and identified Raidenyama Kofun as the tomb of Halfebi, the grandfather of {{ill|Owari no Omi|ja|乎獲居臣}}.<ref>Kuraji Nakamura, "The Musashi Provincial Family Conflict and Large-Scale Mounds," {{ill|Saitama Prefectural Museum of History and Folklore|ja|埼玉県立歴史と民俗の博物館}}.</ref>
== Excavated Artifacts == [[File:雷電山古墳 (東松山市) 出土 円筒埴輪.JPG|thumb|180px|right|{{center|Cylindrical haniwa}}{{small|Displayed at the {{ill|Saitama Prefectural Museum of History and Folklore|ja|埼玉県立歴史と民俗の博物館}}.}}]] Artifacts include {{ill|morning glory-shaped haniwa|ja|朝顔形埴輪|1=Morning glory-shaped haniwa}}, cylindrical haniwa, and stand-shaped haniwa. These artifacts differ significantly from commonly known haniwa, as they lack brush marks typically found on their surfaces. This indicates a manufacturing technique closer to {{ill|haji ware|ja|土師器}}. Evidence of open firing, resulting in blackened surfaces, suggests that these haniwa were made in the early 5th century. These artifacts are currently on display at the {{ill|Saitama Prefectural Museum of History and Folklore|ja|埼玉県立歴史と民俗の博物館}}.

{{Infobox tumulus | name = Raidenyama Kofun | image = {{Image requested|cat=Higashimatsuyama}} | belonging_to = {{ill|Sanzenzuka Kofun Cluster|ja|三千塚古墳群}} | location = {{ill|Ōya, Higashimatsuyama|ja|東松山市大谷}}, {{ill|Higashimatsuyama|ja|東松山市}}, {{ill|Saitama Prefecture|ja|埼玉県}}, Japan | coordinates = {{Coord|36|05|13.8|N|139|23|17.8|E|region:JP|display=inline,title}} | shape = {{ill|Scallop-shaped tumulus|ja|帆立貝形古墳|1=Scallop Kofun}} | size = Total length 85m, height 7m, rear circle diameter 73m, front width 39m | construction_era = Early 5th century | artifacts = {{ill|haniwa|ja|埴輪}} | historic_site_designation = Included in Sanzenzuka Kofun Cluster; designated as a municipal historic site on February 6, 1956<ref name="Higashimatsuyama">{{Cite web|url=http://www.city.higashimatsuyama.lg.jp/kurashi/bunka_sports/bunka/bunkazai/shitei_bunkazai/1350459239838.html|title=List of Cultural Properties|publisher=Higashimatsuyama City|date=2021-07-02|accessdate=2021-11-29}}</ref> | tangible_cultural_properties = Excavated haniwa, designated on March 26, 2004<ref name="Higashimatsuyama"/> | map = {{Location map|Japan Saitama|label=Raidenyama Kofun|lat_deg=36|lat_min=05|lat_sec=13.8|lon_deg=139|lon_min=23|lon_sec=17.8|mark=帆立貝形古墳-北西.svg|marksize=28|float=center|border=none|position=bottom|width=200|caption=Location in Saitama Prefecture}} }}
'''Raidenyama Kofun''' (雷電山古墳) is a {{ill|scallop-shaped tumulus|ja|帆立貝形古墳|1=Scallop Kofun}} located in the {{ill|Sanzenzuka Kofun Cluster|ja|三千塚古墳群}} in {{ill|Higashimatsuyama|ja|東松山市}}, {{ill|Saitama Prefecture|ja|埼玉県}}. It is the oldest tumulus in Saitama Prefecture known to feature {{ill|haniwa|ja|埴輪}}.
== Overview == Constructed atop the 90-meter-high summit of Raidenyama Hill in Ōya, the tumulus was built with earth heaped only on the uppermost tier of the rear circle, while other parts were carved from the natural terrain. The tumulus shape is similar to the {{ill|Otomeyama Kofun|ja|乙女山古墳}} in Nara Prefecture. Excavation in 1984 revealed a three-tiered mound structure, with paving stones on the summit of the front section and four concentric rows of haniwa encircling the tumulus. These haniwa are the oldest found in Saitama Prefecture. A square arrangement of haniwa was also discovered on the rear summit, suggesting the presence of a burial facility beneath. The burial facility is presumed to be either a clay-enclosed burial chamber or a wooden coffin burial.
The site was designated as part of the "Sanzenzuka Kofun Cluster" municipal historic site in 1956, and the excavated haniwa were designated as municipal tangible cultural properties in 2004.
Isao Masuda hypothesized that the origin of the {{ill|Sakitama Kofun Cluster|ja|埼玉古墳群}} lies in the Hiki region and identified Raidenyama Kofun as the tomb of Halfebi, the grandfather of {{ill|Owari no Omi|ja|乎獲居臣}}.<ref>Kuraji Nakamura, "The Musashi Provincial Family Conflict and Large-Scale Mounds," {{ill|Saitama Prefectural Museum of History and Folklore|ja|埼玉県立歴史と民俗の博物館}}.</ref>
== Excavated Artifacts == [[File:雷電山古墳 (東松山市) 出土 円筒埴輪.JPG|thumb|180px|right|{{center|Cylindrical haniwa}}{{small|Displayed at the {{ill|Saitama Prefectural Museum of History and Folklore|ja|埼玉県立歴史と民俗の博物館}}.}}]] Artifacts include {{ill|morning glory-shaped haniwa|ja|朝顔形埴輪|1=Morning glory-shaped haniwa}}, cylindrical haniwa, and stand-shaped haniwa. These artifacts differ significantly from commonly known haniwa, as they lack brush marks typically found on their surfaces. This indicates a manufacturing technique closer to {{ill|haji ware|ja|土師器}}. Evidence of open firing, resulting in blackened surfaces, suggests that these haniwa were made in the early 5th century. These artifacts are currently on display at the {{ill|Saitama Prefectural Museum of History and Folklore|ja|埼玉県立歴史と民俗の博物館}}.
== Notes == {{Reflist}}
== References == * Hiroshi Shiono, *Kofun of Saitama: Hiki and Chichibu*, pp. 67–75, Sakitama Publishing, 2004.
== See Also == * {{ill|List of kofun in Japan|ja|日本の古墳一覧}} * {{ill|List of kofun in Saitama Prefecture|ja|埼玉県の古墳一覧}}
== External Links == * [http://kofun.info/kofun/312 Raidenyama Kofun] - Kofun Map * [http://www.city.higashimatsuyama.lg.jp/kurashi/bunka_sports/bunka/bunkazai/shitei_bunkazai/1350459239838.html List of Cultural Properties] - Higashimatsuyama City

{{Infobox tumulus | name = Raidenyama Kofun | image = {{Image requested|cat=Higashimatsuyama}} | belonging_to = {{ill|Sanzenzuka Kofun Cluster|ja|三千塚古墳群}} | location = {{ill|Ōya, Higashimatsuyama|ja|東松山市大谷}}, {{ill|Higashimatsuyama|ja|東松山市}}, {{ill|Saitama Prefecture|ja|埼玉県}}, Japan | coordinates = {{Coord|36|05|13.8|N|139|23|17.8|E|region:JP|display=inline,title}} | shape = {{ill|Scallop-shaped tumulus|ja|帆立貝形古墳|1=Scallop Kofun}} | size = Total length 85m, height 7m, rear circle diameter 73m, front width 39m | construction_era = Early 5th century | artifacts = {{ill|haniwa|ja|埴輪}} | historic_site_designation = Included in Sanzenzuka Kofun Cluster; designated as a municipal historic site on February 6, 1956<ref name="Higashimatsuyama">{{Cite web|url=http://www.city.higashimatsuyama.lg.jp/kurashi/bunka_sports/bunka/bunkazai/shitei_bunkazai/1350459239838.html|title=List of Cultural Properties|publisher=Higashimatsuyama City|date=2021-07-02|accessdate=2021-11-29}}</ref> | tangible_cultural_properties = Excavated haniwa, designated on March 26, 2004<ref name="Higashimatsuyama"/> | map = {{Location map|Japan Saitama|label=Raidenyama Kofun|lat_deg=36|lat_min=05|lat_sec=13.8|lon_deg=139|lon_min=23|lon_sec=17.8|mark=帆立貝形古墳-北西.svg|marksize=28|float=center|border=none|position=bottom|width=200|caption=Location in Saitama Prefecture}} }}
'''Raidenyama Kofun''' (雷電山古墳) is a {{ill|scallop-shaped tumulus|ja|帆立貝形古墳|1=Scallop Kofun}} located in the {{ill|Sanzenzuka Kofun Cluster|ja|三千塚古墳群}} in {{ill|Higashimatsuyama|ja|東松山市}}, {{ill|Saitama Prefecture|ja|埼玉県}}. It is the oldest tumulus in Saitama Prefecture known to feature {{ill|haniwa|ja|埴輪}}.
== Overview == Constructed atop the 90-meter-high summit of Raidenyama Hill in Ōya, the tumulus was built with earth heaped only on the uppermost tier of the rear circle, while other parts were carved from the natural terrain. The tumulus shape is similar to the {{ill|Otomeyama Kofun|ja|乙女山古墳}} in Nara Prefecture. Excavation in 1984 revealed a three-tiered mound structure, with paving stones on the summit of the front section and four concentric rows of haniwa encircling the tumulus. These haniwa are the oldest found in Saitama Prefecture. A square arrangement of haniwa was also discovered on the rear summit, suggesting the presence of a burial facility beneath. The burial facility is presumed to be either a clay-enclosed burial chamber or a wooden coffin burial.
The site was designated as part of the "Sanzenzuka Kofun Cluster" municipal historic site in 1956, and the excavated haniwa were designated as municipal tangible cultural properties in 2004.
Isao Masuda hypothesized that the origin of the {{ill|Sakitama Kofun Cluster|ja|埼玉古墳群}} lies in the Hiki region and identified Raidenyama Kofun as the tomb of Halfebi, the grandfather of {{ill|Owari no Omi|ja|乎獲居臣}}.<ref>Kuraji Nakamura, "The Musashi Provincial Family Conflict and Large-Scale Mounds," {{ill|Saitama Prefectural Museum of History and Folklore|ja|埼玉県立歴史と民俗の博物館}}.</ref>
== Excavated Artifacts == [[File:雷電山古墳 (東松山市) 出土 円筒埴輪.JPG|thumb|180px|right|{{center|Cylindrical haniwa}}{{small|Displayed at the {{ill|Saitama Prefectural Museum of History and Folklore|ja|埼玉県立歴史と民俗の博物館}}.}}]] Artifacts include {{ill|morning glory-shaped haniwa|ja|朝顔形埴輪|1=Morning glory-shaped haniwa}}, cylindrical haniwa, and stand-shaped haniwa. These artifacts differ significantly from commonly known haniwa, as they lack brush marks typically found on their surfaces. This indicates a manufacturing technique closer to {{ill|haji ware|ja|土師器}}. Evidence of open firing, resulting in blackened surfaces, suggests that these haniwa were made in the early 5th century. These artifacts are currently on display at the {{ill|Saitama Prefectural Museum of History and Folklore|ja|埼玉県立歴史と民俗の博物館}}.
== Notes == {{Reflist}}
== References == * Hiroshi Shiono, *Kofun of Saitama: Hiki and Chichibu*, pp. 67–75, Sakitama Publishing, 2004.
== See Also == * {{ill|List of kofun in Japan|ja|日本の古墳一覧}} * {{ill|List of kofun in Saitama Prefecture|ja|埼玉県の古墳一覧}}

Isao Masuda hypothesized that the origin of the {{ill|Sakitama Kofun Cluster|ja|埼玉古墳群}} lies in the Hiki region and identified Raidenyama Kofun as the tomb of Halfebi, the grandfather of {{ill|Owari no Omi|ja|乎獲居臣}}.<ref>Kuraji Nakamura, "The Musashi Provincial Family Conflict and Large-Scale Mounds," {{ill|Saitama Prefectural Museum of History and Folklore|ja|埼玉県立歴史と民俗の博物館}}.</ref>
== Excavated Artifacts == [[File:雷電山古墳 (東松山市) 出土 円筒埴輪.JPG|thumb|180px|right|{{center|Cylindrical haniwa}}{{small|Displayed at the {{ill|Saitama Prefectural Museum of History and Folklore|ja|埼玉県立歴史と民俗の博物館}}.}}]] Artifacts include {{ill|morning glory-shaped haniwa|ja|朝顔形埴輪|1=Morning glory-shaped haniwa}}, cylindrical haniwa, and stand-shaped haniwa. These artifacts differ significantly from commonly known haniwa, as they lack brush marks typically found on their surfaces. This indicates a manufacturing technique closer to {{ill|haji ware|ja|土師器}}. Evidence of open firing, resulting in blackened surfaces, suggests that these haniwa were made in the early 5th century. These artifacts are currently on display at the {{ill|Saitama Prefectural Museum of History and Folklore|ja|埼玉県立歴史と民俗の博物館}}.
== Notes == {{Reflist}}
== References == * Hiroshi Shiono, *Kofun of Saitama: Hiki and Chichibu*, pp. 67–75, Sakitama Publishing, 2004.

This indicates a manufacturing technique closer to {{ill|haji ware|ja|土師器}}. Evidence of open firing, resulting in blackened surfaces, suggests that these haniwa were made in the early 5th century. These artifacts are currently on display at the {{ill|Saitama Prefectural Museum of History and Folklore|ja|埼玉県立歴史と民俗の博物館}}.

{{Infobox tumulus | name = Raidenyama Kofun | image = {{Image requested|cat=Higashimatsuyama}} | belonging_to = {{ill|Sanzenzuka Kofun Cluster|ja|三千塚古墳群}} | location = {{ill|Ōya, Higashimatsuyama|ja|東松山市大谷}}, {{ill|Higashimatsuyama|ja|東松山市}}, {{ill|Saitama Prefecture|ja|埼玉県}}, Japan | coordinates = {{Coord|36|05|13.8|N|139|23|17.8|E|region:JP|display=inline,title}} | shape = {{ill|Scallop-shaped tumulus|ja|帆立貝形古墳|1=Scallop Kofun}} | size = Total length 85m, height 7m, rear circle diameter 73m, front width 39m | construction_era = Early 5th century | artifacts = {{ill|haniwa|ja|埴輪}} | historic_site_designation = Included in Sanzenzuka Kofun Cluster; designated as a municipal historic site on February 6, 1956<ref name="Higashimatsuyama">{{Cite web|url=http://www.city.higashimatsuyama.lg.jp/kurashi/bunka_sports/bunka/bunkazai/shitei_bunkazai/1350459239838.html|title=List of Cultural Properties|publisher=Higashimatsuyama City|date=2021-07-02|accessdate=2021-11-29}}</ref> | tangible_cultural_properties = Excavated haniwa, designated on March 26, 2004<ref name="Higashimatsuyama"/> | map = {{Location map|Japan Saitama|label=Raidenyama Kofun|lat_deg=36|lat_min=05|lat_sec=13.8|lon_deg=139|lon_min=23|lon_sec=17.8|mark=帆立貝形古墳-北西.svg|marksize=28|float=center|border=none|position=bottom|width=200|caption=Location in Saitama Prefecture}} }}
'''Raidenyama Kofun''' (雷電山古墳) is a {{ill|scallop-shaped tumulus|ja|帆立貝形古墳|1=Scallop Kofun}} located in the {{ill|Sanzenzuka Kofun Cluster|ja|三千塚古墳群}} in {{ill|Higashimatsuyama|ja|東松山市}}, {{ill|Saitama Prefecture|ja|埼玉県}}. It is the oldest tumulus in Saitama Prefecture known to feature {{ill|haniwa|ja|埴輪}}.
== Overview == Constructed atop the 90-meter-high summit of Raidenyama Hill in Ōya, the tumulus was built with earth heaped only on the uppermost tier of the rear circle, while other parts were carved from the natural terrain. The tumulus shape is similar to the {{ill|Otomeyama Kofun|ja|乙女山古墳}} in Nara Prefecture. Excavation in 1984 revealed a three-tiered mound structure, with paving stones on the summit of the front section and four concentric rows of haniwa encircling the tumulus. These haniwa are the oldest found in Saitama Prefecture. A square arrangement of haniwa was also discovered on the rear summit, suggesting the presence of a burial facility beneath. The burial facility is presumed to be either a clay-enclosed burial chamber or a wooden coffin burial.
The site was designated as part of the "Sanzenzuka Kofun Cluster" municipal historic site in 1956, and the excavated haniwa were designated as municipal tangible cultural properties in 2004.
Isao Masuda hypothesized that the origin of the {{ill|Sakitama Kofun Cluster|ja|埼玉古墳群}} lies in the Hiki region and identified Raidenyama Kofun as the tomb of Halfebi, the grandfather of {{ill|Owari no Omi|ja|乎獲居臣}}.<ref>Kuraji Nakamura, "The Musashi Provincial Family Conflict and Large-Scale Mounds," {{ill|Saitama Prefectural Museum of History and Folklore|ja|埼玉県立歴史と民俗の博物館}}.</ref>
== Excavated Artifacts == [[File:雷電山古墳 (東松山市) 出土 円筒埴輪.JPG|thumb|180px|right|{{center|Cylindrical haniwa}}{{small|Displayed at the {{ill|Saitama Prefectural Museum of History and Folklore|ja|埼玉県立歴史と民俗の博物館}}.}}]] Artifacts include {{ill|morning glory-shaped haniwa|ja|朝顔形埴輪|1=Morning glory-shaped haniwa}}, cylindrical haniwa, and stand-shaped haniwa. These artifacts differ significantly from commonly known haniwa, as they lack brush marks typically found on their surfaces. This indicates a manufacturing technique closer to {{ill|haji ware|ja|土師器}}. Evidence of open firing, resulting in blackened surfaces, suggests that these haniwa were made in the early 5th century. These artifacts are currently on display at the {{ill|Saitama Prefectural Museum of History and Folklore|ja|埼玉県立歴史と民俗の博物館}}.
== Notes == {{Reflist}}
== References == * Hiroshi Shiono, *Kofun of Saitama: Hiki and Chichibu*, pp. 67–75, Sakitama Publishing, 2004. == See Also! == * {{ill|List of kofun in Japan|ja|日本の古墳一覧}} * {{ill|List of kofun in Saitama Prefecture|ja|埼玉県の古墳一覧}}
== External Links == * [http://kofun.info/kofun/312 Raidenyama Kofun] - Kofun Map * [http://www.city.higashimatsuyama.lg.jp/kurashi/bunka_sports/bunka/bunkazai/shitei_bunkazai/1350459239838.html List of Cultural Properties] - Higashimatsuyama City

revision.text:
{{Infobox tumulus | name = Raidenyama Kofun | image = {{Image requested|cat=Higashimatsuyama}} | belonging_to = {{ill|Sanzenzuka Kofun Cluster|ja|三千塚古墳群}} | location = {{ill|Ōya, Higashimatsuyama|ja|東松山市大谷}}, {{ill|Higashimatsuyama|ja|東松山市}}, {{ill|Saitama Prefecture|ja|埼玉県}}, Japan | coordinates = {{Coord|36|05|13.8|N|139|23|17.8|E|region:JP|display=inline,title}} | shape = {{ill|Scallop-shaped tumulus|ja|帆立貝形古墳|1=Scallop Kofun}} | area = Total length 85m, height 7m, rear circle diameter 73m, front width 39m | construction_era = Early 5th century | artifacts = {{ill|haniwa|ja|埴輪}} | historic_site_designation = Included in Sanzenzuka Kofun Cluster; designated as a municipal historic site on February 6, 1956<ref name="Higashimatsuyama">{{Cite web|url=http://www.city.higashimatsuyama.lg.jp/kurashi/bunka_sports/bunka/bunkazai/shitei_bunkazai/1350459239838.html|title=List of Cultural Properties|publisher=Higashimatsuyama City|date=2021-07-02|accessdate=2021-11-29}}</ref> | tangible_cultural_properties = Excavated haniwa, designated on March 26, 2004<ref name="Higashimatsuyama"/> | map = {{Location map|Japan Saitama|label=Raidenyama Kofun|lat_deg=36|lat_min=05|lat_sec=13.8|lon_deg=139|lon_min=23|lon_sec=17.8|mark=帆立貝形古墳-北西.svg|marksize=28|float=center|border=none|position=bottom|width=200|caption=Location in Saitama Prefecture}} }}
'''Raidenyama Kofun''' (雷電山古墳) is a {{ill|scallop-shaped tumulus|ja|帆立貝形古墳|1=Scallop Kofun}} located in the {{ill|Sanzenzuka Kofun Cluster|ja|三千塚古墳群}} in {{ill|Higashimatsuyama|ja|東松山市}}, {{ill|Saitama Prefecture|ja|埼玉県}}. It is the oldest tumulus in Saitama Prefecture known to feature {{ill|haniwa|ja|埴輪}}.
== Overview == Constructed atop the 90-meter-high summit of Raidenyama Hill in Ōya, the tumulus was built with earth heaped only on the uppermost tier of the rear circle, while other parts were carved from the natural terrain. The tumulus shape is similar to the {{ill|Otomeyama Kofun|ja|乙女山古墳}} in Nara Prefecture. Excavation in 1984 revealed a three-tiered mound structure, with paving stones on the summit of the front section and four concentric rows of haniwa encircling the tumulus. These haniwa are the oldest found in Saitama Prefecture. A square arrangement of haniwa was also discovered on the rear summit, suggesting the presence of a burial facility beneath. The burial facility is presumed to be either a clay-enclosed burial chamber or a wooden coffin burial.
The site was designated as part of the "Sanzenzuka Kofun Cluster" municipal historic site in 1956, and the excavated haniwa were designated as municipal tangible cultural properties in 2004.
Isao Masuda hypothesized that the origin of the {{ill|Sakitama Kofun Cluster|ja|埼玉古墳群}} lies in the Hiki region and identified Raidenyama Kofun as the tomb of Halfebi, the grandfather of {{ill|Owari no Omi|ja|乎獲居臣}}.<ref>Kuraji Nakamura, "The Musashi Provincial Family Conflict and Large-Scale Mounds," {{ill|Saitama Prefectural Museum of History and Folklore|ja|埼玉県立歴史と民俗の博物館}}.</ref>
== Excavated Artifacts == [[File:雷電山古墳 (東松山市) 出土 円筒埴輪.JPG|thumb|180px|right|{{center|Cylindrical haniwa}}{{small|Displayed at the {{ill|Saitama Prefectural Museum of History and Folklore|ja|埼玉県立歴史と民俗の博物館}}.}}]] Artifacts include {{ill|morning glory-shaped haniwa|ja|朝顔形埴輪|1=Morning glory-shaped haniwa}}, cylindrical haniwa, and stand-shaped haniwa. These artifacts differ significantly from commonly known haniwa, as they lack brush marks typically found on their surfaces. This indicates a manufacturing technique closer to {{ill|haji ware|ja|土師器|1=Haji pottery}}. Evidence of open firing, resulting in blackened surfaces, suggests that these haniwa were made in the early 5th century. These artifacts are currently on display at the {{ill|Saitama Prefectural Museum of History and Folklore|ja|埼玉県立歴史と民俗の博物館}}.
== Notes == {{Reflist}}
== References == * Hiroshi Shiono, *Kofun of Saitama: Hiki and Chichibu*, pp. 67–75, Sakitama Publishing, 2004. == See Also == * {{ill|List of kofun in Japan|ja|日本の古墳一覧}} * {{ill|List of kofun in Saitama Prefecture|ja|埼玉県の古墳一覧}}
== External Links == * [http://kofun.info/kofun/312 Raidenyama Kofun] - Kofun Map * [http://www.city.higashimatsuyama.lg.jp/kurashi/bunka_sports/bunka/bunkazai/shitei_bunkazai/1350459239838.html List of Cultural Properties] - Higashimatsuyama City

The site was designated as part of the "Sanzenzuka Kofun Cluster" municipal historic site in 1956, and the excavated haniwa were designated as municipal tangible cultural properties in 2004.
Isao Masuda hypothesized that the origin of the {{ill|Sakitama Kofun Cluster|ja|埼玉古墳群}} lies in the Hiki region and identified Raidenyama Kofun as the tomb of Halfebi, the grandfather of {{ill|Owari no Omi|ja|乎獲居臣}}.<ref>Kuraji Nakamura, "The Musashi Provincial Family Conflict and Large-Scale Mounds," {{ill|Saitama Prefectural Museum of History and Folklore|ja|埼玉県立歴史と民俗の博物館}}.</ref>
== Excavated Artifacts == [[File:雷電山古墳 (東松山市) 出土 円筒埴輪.JPG|thumb|180px|right|{{center|Cylindrical haniwa}}{{small|Displayed at the {{ill|Saitama Prefectural Museum of History and Folklore|ja|埼玉県立歴史と民俗の博物館}}.}}]] Artifacts include {{ill|morning glory-shaped haniwa|ja|朝顔形埴輪|1=Morning glory-shaped haniwa}}, cylindrical haniwa, and stand-shaped haniwa. These artifacts differ significantly from commonly known haniwa, as they lack brush marks typically found on their surfaces. This indicates a manufacturing technique closer to {{ill|haji ware|ja|土師器|1=Haji pottery}}. Evidence of open firing, resulting in blackened surfaces, suggests that these haniwa were made in the early 5th century. These artifacts are currently on display at the {{ill|Saitama Prefectural Museum of History and Folklore|ja|埼玉県立歴史と民俗の博物館}}.
== Notes == {{Reflist}}
== References == * Hiroshi Shiono, *Kofun of Saitama: Hiki and Chichibu*, pp. 67–75, Sakitama Publishing, 2004. == See Also == * {{ill|List of kofun in Japan|ja|日本の古墳一覧}} * {{ill|List of kofun in Saitama Prefecture|ja|埼玉県の古墳一覧}}

It is the oldest tumulus in Saitama Prefecture known to feature {{ill|haniwa|ja|埴輪|12=draft}}.
== Overview == Constructed atop the 90-meter-high summit of Raidenyama Hill in Ōya, the tumulus was built with earth heaped only on the uppermost tier of the rear circle, while other parts were carved from the natural terrain. The tumulus shape is similar to the {{ill|Otomeyama Kofun|ja|乙女山古墳|12=draft}} in Nara Prefecture. Excavation in 1984 revealed a three-tiered mound structure, with paving stones on the summit of the front section and four concentric rows of haniwa encircling the tumulus. These haniwa are the oldest found in Saitama Prefecture. A square arrangement of haniwa was also discovered on the rear summit, suggesting the presence of a burial facility beneath. The burial facility is presumed to be either a clay-enclosed burial chamber or a wooden coffin burial.
The site was designated as part of the "Sanzenzuka Kofun Cluster" municipal historic site in 1956, and the excavated haniwa were designated as municipal tangible cultural properties in 2004.
Isao Masuda hypothesized that the origin of the {{ill|Sakitama Kofun Cluster|ja|埼玉古墳群|12=draft}} lies in the Hiki region and identified Raidenyama Kofun as the tomb of Halfebi, the grandfather of {{ill|Owari no Omi|ja|乎獲居臣|12=draft}}.<ref>Kuraji Nakamura, "The Musashi Provincial Family Conflict and Large-Scale Mounds," {{ill|Saitama Prefectural Museum of History and Folklore|ja|埼玉県立歴史と民俗の博物館|12=draft}}.</ref>
== Excavated Artifacts == [[File:雷電山古墳 (東松山市) 出土 円筒埴輪.JPG|thumb|180px|right|{{center|Cylindrical haniwa}}{{small|Displayed at the {{ill|Saitama Prefectural Museum of History and Folklore|ja|埼玉県立歴史と民俗の博物館|12=draft}}.}}]] Artifacts include {{ill|morning glory-shaped haniwa|ja|朝顔形埴輪|1=Morning glory-shaped haniwa|12=draft}}, cylindrical haniwa, and stand-shaped haniwa. These artifacts differ significantly from commonly known haniwa, as they lack brush marks typically found on their surfaces. This indicates a manufacturing technique closer to {{ill|haji ware|ja|土師器|1=Haji pottery|12=draft}}. Evidence of open firing, resulting in blackened surfaces, suggests that these haniwa were made in the early 5th century. These artifacts are currently on display at the {{ill|Saitama Prefectural Museum of History and Folklore|ja|埼玉県立歴史と民俗の博物館|12=draft}}.
== Notes == {{Reflist}}
== References == * Hiroshi Shiono, *Kofun of Saitama: Hiki and Chichibu*, pp. 67–75, Sakitama Publishing, 2004. == See Also == * {{ill|List of kofun in Japan|ja|日本の古墳一覧|12=draft}} * {{ill|List of kofun in Saitama Prefecture|ja|埼玉県の古墳一覧|12=draft}}
== External Links == * [http://kofun.info/kofun/312 Raidenyama Kofun] - Kofun Map * [http://www.city.higashimatsuyama.lg.jp/kurashi/bunka_sports/bunka/bunkazai/shitei_bunkazai/1350459239838.html List of Cultural Properties] - Higashimatsuyama City

revision.text:
{{単一の出典|date=2013年11月7日 (木) 17:00 (UTC)}}
{{日本の古墳 |名称=雷電山古墳 |画像={{画像募集中|cat=東松山市}} |所属=[[三千塚古墳群]] |所在地=埼玉県東松山市大谷 |位置 = {{ウィキ座標2段度分秒|36|05|13.8|N|139|23|17.8|E|region:JP|display=inline,title}} |形状=帆立貝形古墳 |規模=全長85m、高さ7m、後円部径73m、前方部幅39m |築造年代=5世紀前半 |出土品=埴輪 |史跡指定=三千塚古墳群、[[1956年]]（昭和31年）2月6日市指定<ref name=東松山>{{Cite web|和書|url=http://www.city.higashimatsuyama.lg.jp/kurashi/bunka_sports/bunka/bunkazai/shitei_bunkazai/1350459239838.html|title=文化財一覧|work=東松山市|accessdate=2021-11-29|date=2021-07-02}}</ref> |有形文化財=出土埴輪、[[2004年]]（平成16年）3月26日市指定<ref name=東松山/> |地図2 = {{Location map|Japan Saitama|label=雷電山古墳|lat_deg=36|lat_min=05|lat_sec=13.8|lon_deg=139|lon_min=23|lon_sec=17.8|mark=帆立貝形古墳-北西.svg|marksize=28|float=center|border=none|position=bottom|width=200|caption=埼玉県内の位置}} {{maplink2|type=point|frame=yes|plain=yes|zoom=12|frame-align=center|frame-width=200|coord={{coord|36|05|13.8|N|139|23|17.8|E}}}} |特記事項=1976年（昭和51年）10月1日「埼玉県選定重要遺跡」選定<ref>{{Cite web|和書|url=https://www.pref.saitama.lg.jp/f2216/bunkazai-kensu.html|title=県選定重要遺跡|work=埼玉県|date=2021-10-18|accessdate=2021-11-29}}</ref> }}
'''雷電山古墳'''（らいでんやまこふん）は、[[埼玉県]][[東松山市]]の[[三千塚古墳群]]内にある[[帆立貝形古墳]]である。[[埴輪]]を持つ埼玉県最古の古墳。
== 概要 == 大谷の丘陵上、標高90メートルの雷電山山頂に築造された。後円部の最上段のみ盛り土がされ、それ以外の部分は地山を削り出して造成されている。墳丘の形状は奈良・[[乙女山古墳]]に類似する。1984年（昭和59年）の調査で、墳丘は三段構成であること、前方部頂部に[[葺石]]が施されていること、四重の埴輪列が巡ることが明らかになった。埼玉県で最も古い埴輪の出土例である。後円部墳頂にも方形の埴輪列が確認されており、その中心に埋葬施設が存在すると思われる。主体部は[[粘土槨]]か木棺直葬と見られている。
古墳群は1956年（昭和31年）に「三千塚古墳群」として市の[[史跡]]に指定され、出土した埴輪も2004年（平成16年）に市の[[有形文化財]]（考古資料）に指定されている。
[[増田一郎]]は、突如現れる[[埼玉古墳群]]の故地を[[比企]]の地に比定しており、雷電山古墳は[[乎獲居臣]]の祖父である半弖比の墓であるとした<ref>中村倉司「埼玉丸慕山古墳と大里甲山古墳 ー武蔵国造家内紛と大型円墳一[https://sakitama-muse.spec.ed.jp/cabinets/cabinet_files/download/308/9407c74bd90fc866401e895534feb62b?frame_id=338]」</ref>。
== 出土品 == [[ファイル:雷電山古墳 (東松山市) 出土 円筒埴輪.JPG|thumb|180px|right|{{center|円筒埴輪}}{{small|[[埼玉県立歴史と民俗の博物館]]展示。}}]] [[朝顔形埴輪]]、[[円筒埴輪]]、器台形埴輪が確認された。これらの埴輪は、一般に知られているそれとは別物ともいえる特異なものである。いずれも表面の調整にハケメ工具が用いられておらず、これは形状が特異なばかりでなく、埴輪というよりむしろ[[土師器]]に近い技術で作られているといえる。野焼き製作を示す黒斑が認められることから5世紀前半の製作と推定されている。これらの埴輪は、現在[[埼玉県立歴史と民俗の博物館]]に展示されている。
==脚注== {{Reflist}}
== 参考文献 == {{参照方法|date=2013年11月7日 (木) 17:00 (UTC)|section=3}} * 塩野博『埼玉の古墳 比企・秩父』 pp.67-75、さきたま出版会、2004年
== 関連項目 == * [[日本の古墳一覧]] * [[埼玉県の古墳一覧]]
== 外部リンク == * [http://kofun.info/kofun/312 雷電山古墳] - 古墳マップ * [http://www.city.higashimatsuyama.lg.jp/kurashi/bunka_sports/bunka/bunkazai/shitei_bunkazai/1350459239838.html 文化財一覧] - 東松山市
{{japanese-history-stub}} {{デフォルトソート:らいてんやまこふん}} [[Category:埼玉県の古墳]] [[Category:東松山市の歴史]] [[Category:帆立貝形古墳]]

}}

'''Raidenyama Kofun''' (雷電山古墳) is a {{ill|scallop-shaped tumulus|ja|帆立貝形古墳|1=scallop-shaped kofun|qq=draft|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/scallop-shaped tumulus|13=User:Immanuelle/scallop-shaped kofun|qq=|comment=enwiki is a redirect}} located in the {{ill|Sanchinzuka Kofun Group|lt=Sanchinzuka Kofun Group|lt=Sanzenzuka Kofun Cluster|ja|三千塚古墳群|qq=draft|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Sanzenzuka Kofun Cluster|qq=}} in [[:en:Higashimatsuyama, Saitama|Higashimatsuyama]], [[:en:Saitama Prefecture|Saitama Prefecture]]. It is the oldest tumulus in Saitama Prefecture known to feature [[:en:Haniwa|haniwa]].

== Overview ==
Constructed atop the 90-meter-high summit of Raidenyama Hill in Ōya, the tumulus was built with earth heaped only on the uppermost tier of the rear circle, while other parts were carved from the natural terrain. The tumulus shape is similar to the [[:en:Otomeyama Kofun|Otomeyama Kofun]] in Nara Prefecture. Excavation in 1984 revealed a three-tiered mound structure, with paving stones on the summit of the front section and four concentric rows of haniwa encircling the tumulus. These haniwa are the oldest found in Saitama Prefecture. A square arrangement of haniwa was also discovered on the rear summit, suggesting the presence of a burial facility beneath. The burial facility is presumed to be either a clay-enclosed burial chamber or a wooden coffin burial.

The site was designated as part of the "Sanzenzuka Kofun Cluster" municipal historic site in 1956, and the excavated haniwa were designated as municipal tangible cultural properties in 2004.

Isao Masuda hypothesized that the origin of the [[:en:Sakitama Kofun Cluster|Sakitama Kofun Cluster]] lies in the Hiki region and identified Raidenyama Kofun as the tomb of Halfebi, the grandfather of {{ill|Owari no Omi|ja|乎獲居臣|qq=draft|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Owari no Omi|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}}.<ref>Kuraji Nakamura, "The Musashi Provincial Family Conflict and Large-Scale Mounds," [[:en:Saitama Prefectural Museum of History and Folklore|Saitama Prefectural Museum of History and Folklore]].</ref>

== Excavated Artifacts ==
[[File:雷電山古墳 (東松山市) 出土 円筒埴輪.JPG|thumb|180px|right|{{center|Cylindrical haniwa}}{{small|Displayed at the [[:en:Saitama Prefectural Museum of History and Folklore|Saitama Prefectural Museum of History and Folklore]].}}]]
Artifacts include {{ill|morning glory-shaped haniwa|ja|朝顔形埴輪|1=Morning glory-shaped haniwa|qq=draft|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/morning glory-shaped haniwa|13=User:Immanuelle/Morning glory-shaped haniwa|qq=}}, cylindrical haniwa, and stand-shaped haniwa. These artifacts differ significantly from commonly known haniwa, as they lack brush marks typically found on their surfaces. This indicates a manufacturing technique closer to [[:en:Haji pottery|Haji pottery]]. Evidence of open firing, resulting in blackened surfaces, suggests that these haniwa were made in the early 5th century. These artifacts are currently on display at the [[:en:Saitama Prefectural Museum of History and Folklore|Saitama Prefectural Museum of History and Folklore]].

'''Raidenyama Kofun''' (雷電山古墳) is a {{ill|scallop-shaped tumulus|ja|帆立貝形古墳|1=scallop-shaped kofun|qq=draft|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/scallop-shaped tumulus|13=User:Immanuelle/scallop-shaped kofun|qq=|comment=enwiki is a redirect}} located in the {{ill|Sanchinzuka Kofun Group|lt=Sanchinzuka Kofun Group|lt=Sanzenzuka Kofun Cluster|ja|三千塚古墳群|qq=draft|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Sanzenzuka Kofun Cluster|qq=}} in [[:en:Higashimatsuyama, Saitama|Higashimatsuyama]], [[:en:Saitama Prefecture|Saitama Prefecture]]. It is the oldest tumulus in Saitama Prefecture known to feature [[:en:Haniwa|haniwa]].

== Overview ==
Constructed atop the 90-meter-high summit of Raidenyama Hill in Ōya, the tumulus was built with earth heaped only on the uppermost tier of the rear circle, while other parts were carved from the natural terrain. The tumulus shape is similar to the [[:en:Otomeyama Kofun|Otomeyama Kofun]] in Nara Prefecture. Excavation in 1984 revealed a three-tiered mound structure, with paving stones on the summit of the front section and four concentric rows of haniwa encircling the tumulus. These haniwa are the oldest found in Saitama Prefecture. A square arrangement of haniwa was also discovered on the rear summit, suggesting the presence of a burial facility beneath. The burial facility is presumed to be either a clay-enclosed burial chamber or a wooden coffin burial.

The site was designated as part of the "Sanzenzuka Kofun Cluster" municipal historic site in 1956, and the excavated haniwa were designated as municipal tangible cultural properties in 2004.

Isao Masuda hypothesized that the origin of the [[:en:Sakitama Kofun Cluster|Sakitama Kofun Cluster]] lies in the Hiki region and identified Raidenyama Kofun as the tomb of Halfebi, the grandfather of {{ill|Owari no Omi|ja|乎獲居臣|qq=draft|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Owari no Omi|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}}.<ref>Kuraji Nakamura, "The Musashi Provincial Family Conflict and Large-Scale Mounds," [[:en:Saitama Prefectural Museum of History and Folklore|Saitama Prefectural Museum of History and Folklore]].</ref>

== Excavated Artifacts ==
[[File:雷電山古墳 (東松山市) 出土 円筒埴輪.JPG|thumb|180px|right|{{center|Cylindrical haniwa}}{{small|Displayed at the [[:en:Saitama Prefectural Museum of History and Folklore|Saitama Prefectural Museum of History and Folklore]].}}]]
Artifacts include {{ill|morning glory-shaped haniwa|ja|朝顔形埴輪|1=Morning glory-shaped haniwa|qq=draft|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/morning glory-shaped haniwa|13=User:Immanuelle/Morning glory-shaped haniwa|qq=}}, cylindrical haniwa, and stand-shaped haniwa. These artifacts differ significantly from commonly known haniwa, as they lack brush marks typically found on their surfaces. This indicates a manufacturing technique closer to [[:en:Haji pottery|Haji pottery]]. Evidence of open firing, resulting in blackened surfaces, suggests that these haniwa were made in the early 5th century. These artifacts are currently on display at the [[:en:Saitama Prefectural Museum of History and Folklore|Saitama Prefectural Museum of History and Folklore]].
