'''Raidenyama Kofun''' (雷電山古墳) is a {{ill|scallop-shaped tumulus|ja|帆立貝形古墳|1=scallop-shaped kofun|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/scallop-shaped tumulus|13=User:Immanuelle/scallop-shaped kofun|comment=enwiki is a redirect}} located in the {{ill|Sanchinzuka Kofun Group|lt=Sanchinzuka Kofun Group|lt=Sanzenzuka Kofun Cluster|ja|三千塚古墳群|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Sanzenzuka Kofun Cluster|qq=}} in [[:en:Higashimatsuyama, Saitama|Higashimatsuyama]], [[:en:Saitama Prefecture|Saitama Prefecture]]. It is the oldest tumulus in Saitama Prefecture known to feature [[:en:Haniwa|haniwa]].

== Overview ==
Constructed atop the 90-meter-high summit of Raidenyama Hill in Ōya, the tumulus was built with earth heaped only on the uppermost tier of the rear circle, while other parts were carved from the natural terrain. The tumulus shape is similar to the [[:en:Otomeyama Kofun|Otomeyama Kofun]] in Nara Prefecture. Excavation in 1984 revealed a three-tiered mound structure, with paving stones on the summit of the front section and four concentric rows of haniwa encircling the tumulus. These haniwa are the oldest found in Saitama Prefecture. A square arrangement of haniwa was also discovered on the rear summit, suggesting the presence of a burial facility beneath. The burial facility is presumed to be either a clay-enclosed burial chamber or a wooden coffin burial.

The site was designated as part of the "Sanzenzuka Kofun Cluster" municipal historic site in 1956, and the excavated haniwa were designated as municipal tangible cultural properties in 2004.

Isao Masuda hypothesized that the origin of the [[:en:Sakitama Kofun Cluster|Sakitama Kofun Cluster]] lies in the Hiki region and identified Raidenyama Kofun as the tomb of Halfebi, the grandfather of {{ill|Owari no Omi|ja|乎獲居臣|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Owari no Omi|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}}.<ref>Kuraji Nakamura, "The Musashi Provincial Family Conflict and Large-Scale Mounds," [[:en:Saitama Prefectural Museum of History and Folklore|Saitama Prefectural Museum of History and Folklore]].</ref>

== Excavated Artifacts ==
[[File:雷電山古墳 (東松山市) 出土 円筒埴輪.JPG|thumb|180px|right|{{center|Cylindrical haniwa}}{{small|Displayed at the [[:en:Saitama Prefectural Museum of History and Folklore|Saitama Prefectural Museum of History and Folklore]].}}]]
Artifacts include {{ill|morning glory-shaped haniwa|ja|朝顔形埴輪|1=Morning glory-shaped haniwa|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/morning glory-shaped haniwa|13=User:Immanuelle/Morning glory-shaped haniwa|qq=}}, cylindrical haniwa, and stand-shaped haniwa. These artifacts differ significantly from commonly known haniwa, as they lack brush marks typically found on their surfaces. This indicates a manufacturing technique closer to [[:en:Haji pottery|Haji pottery]]. Evidence of open firing, resulting in blackened surfaces, suggests that these haniwa were made in the early 5th century. These artifacts are currently on display at the [[:en:Saitama Prefectural Museum of History and Folklore|Saitama Prefectural Museum of History and Folklore]].

<!--interwikis from wikidata-->[[ja:雷電山古墳]] {{afc comment|Wikidata entry [[da:d:Q16768352]] Wikidata name: (none) [[User:Immanuelle|Immanuelle]] ([[User talk:Immanuelle|talk]]) 05:24, 12 June 2025 (UTC)}} {{afc comment|Wikidata entry (none) Wikidata name: (none) [[User:Immanuelle|Immanuelle]] ([[User talk:Immanuelle|talk]]) 08:55, 11 June 2025 (UTC)}} {{Infobox tumulus | name = Raidenyama Kofun | image = {{Image requested|cat=Higashimatsuyama}} | belonging_to = {{ill|Sanchinzuka Kofun Group|lt=Sanchinzuka Kofun Group|lt=Sanzenzuka Kofun Cluster|ja|三千塚古墳群|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Sanzenzuka Kofun Cluster|qq=}} | location = {{ill|Ōya, Higashimatsuyama|ja|東松山市大谷|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ōya, Higashimatsuyama|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}}, [[:en:Higashimatsuyama, Saitama|Higashimatsuyama]], [[:en:Saitama Prefecture|Saitama Prefecture]], Japan | coordinates = {{Coord|36|05|13.8|N|139|23|17.8|E|region:JP|display=inline,title}} | shape = {{ill|Scallop-shaped tumulus|ja|帆立貝形古墳|1=scallop-shaped kofun|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Scallop-shaped tumulus|13=User:Immanuelle/scallop-shaped kofun|comment=enwiki is a redirect}} | area = Total length 85m, height 7m, rear circle diameter 73m, front width 39m | construction_era = Early 5th century | artifacts = [[:en:Haniwa|haniwa]] | historic_site_designation = Included in Sanzenzuka Kofun Cluster; designated as a municipal historic site on February 6, 1956<ref name="Higashimatsuyama">{{Cite web|url=http://www.city.higashimatsuyama.lg.jp/kurashi/bunka_sports/bunka/bunkazai/shitei_bunkazai/1350459239838.html|title=List of Cultural Properties|publisher=Higashimatsuyama City|date=2021-07-02|accessdate=2021-11-29}}</ref> | tangible_cultural_properties = Excavated haniwa, designated on March 26, 2004<ref name="Higashimatsuyama"/> | map = {{Location map|Japan Saitama|label=Raidenyama Kofun|lat_deg=36|lat_min=05|lat_sec=13.8|lon_deg=139|lon_min=23|lon_sec=17.8|mark=帆立貝形古墳-北西.svg|marksize=28|float=center|border=none|position=bottom|width=200|caption=Location in Saitama Prefecture}} }}
'''Raidenyama Kofun''' (雷電山古墳) is a {{ill|scallop-shaped tumulus|ja|帆立貝形古墳|1=scallop-shaped kofun|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/scallop-shaped tumulus|13=User:Immanuelle/scallop-shaped kofun|comment=enwiki is a redirect}} located in the {{ill|Sanchinzuka Kofun Group|lt=Sanchinzuka Kofun Group|lt=Sanzenzuka Kofun Cluster|ja|三千塚古墳群|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Sanzenzuka Kofun Cluster|qq=}} in [[:en:Higashimatsuyama, Saitama|Higashimatsuyama]], [[:en:Saitama Prefecture|Saitama Prefecture]]. It is the oldest tumulus in Saitama Prefecture known to feature [[:en:Haniwa|haniwa]].
== Overview == Constructed atop the 90-meter-high summit of Raidenyama Hill in Ōya, the tumulus was built with earth heaped only on the uppermost tier of the rear circle, while other parts were carved from the natural terrain. The tumulus shape is similar to the [[:en:Otomeyama Kofun|Otomeyama Kofun]] in Nara Prefecture. Excavation in 1984 revealed a three-tiered mound structure, with paving stones on the summit of the front section and four concentric rows of haniwa encircling the tumulus. These haniwa are the oldest found in Saitama Prefecture. A square arrangement of haniwa was also discovered on the rear summit, suggesting the presence of a burial facility beneath. The burial facility is presumed to be either a clay-enclosed burial chamber or a wooden coffin burial.
The site was designated as part of the "Sanzenzuka Kofun Cluster" municipal historic site in 1956, and the excavated haniwa were designated as municipal tangible cultural properties in 2004.
Isao Masuda hypothesized that the origin of the [[:en:Sakitama Kofun Cluster|Sakitama Kofun Cluster]] lies in the Hiki region and identified Raidenyama Kofun as the tomb of Halfebi, the grandfather of {{ill|Owari no Omi|ja|乎獲居臣|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Owari no Omi|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}}.<ref>Kuraji Nakamura, "The Musashi Provincial Family Conflict and Large-Scale Mounds," [[:en:Saitama Prefectural Museum of History and Folklore|Saitama Prefectural Museum of History and Folklore]].</ref>
== Excavated Artifacts == [[File:雷電山古墳 (東松山市) 出土 円筒埴輪.JPG|thumb|180px|right|{{center|Cylindrical haniwa}}{{small|Displayed at the [[:en:Saitama Prefectural Museum of History and Folklore|Saitama Prefectural Museum of History and Folklore]].}}]] Artifacts include {{ill|morning glory-shaped haniwa|ja|朝顔形埴輪|1=Morning glory-shaped haniwa|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/morning glory-shaped haniwa|13=User:Immanuelle/Morning glory-shaped haniwa|qq=}}, cylindrical haniwa, and stand-shaped haniwa. These artifacts differ significantly from commonly known haniwa, as they lack brush marks typically found on their surfaces. This indicates a manufacturing technique closer to [[:en:Haji pottery|Haji pottery]]. Evidence of open firing, resulting in blackened surfaces, suggests that these haniwa were made in the early 5th century. These artifacts are currently on display at the [[:en:Saitama Prefectural Museum of History and Folklore|Saitama Prefectural Museum of History and Folklore]].
== Notes == {{Reflist}}
== References == * Hiroshi Shiono, *Kofun of Saitama: Hiki and Chichibu*, pp. 67–75, Sakitama Publishing, 2004. == See Also == * {{ill|List of kofun in Japan|ja|日本の古墳一覧|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/List of kofun in Japan|1=list of kofun|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11505809}} * {{ill|List of kofun in Saitama Prefecture|ja|埼玉県の古墳一覧|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/List of kofun in Saitama Prefecture|1=List of Kofun in Saitama Prefecture|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11426533}}

designated as a municipal historic site on February 6, 1956<ref name="Higashimatsuyama">{{Cite web|url=http://www.city.higashimatsuyama.lg.jp/kurashi/bunka_sports/bunka/bunkazai/shitei_bunkazai/1350459239838.html|title=List of Cultural Properties|publisher=Higashimatsuyama City|date=2021-07-02|accessdate=2021-11-29}}</ref> | tangible_cultural_properties = Excavated haniwa, designated on March 26, 2004<ref name="Higashimatsuyama"/> | map = {{Location map|Japan Saitama|label=Raidenyama Kofun|lat_deg=36|lat_min=05|lat_sec=13.8|lon_deg=139|lon_min=23|lon_sec=17.8|mark=帆立貝形古墳-北西.svg|marksize=28|float=center|border=none|position=bottom|width=200|caption=Location in Saitama Prefecture}} }}
'''Raidenyama Kofun''' (雷電山古墳) is a {{ill|scallop-shaped tumulus|ja|帆立貝形古墳|1=scallop-shaped kofun|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/scallop-shaped tumulus|13=User:Immanuelle/scallop-shaped kofun|comment=enwiki is a redirect}} located in the {{ill|Sanchinzuka Kofun Group|lt=Sanchinzuka Kofun Group|lt=Sanzenzuka Kofun Cluster|ja|三千塚古墳群|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Sanzenzuka Kofun Cluster|qq=}} in [[:en:Higashimatsuyama, Saitama|Higashimatsuyama]], [[:en:Saitama Prefecture|Saitama Prefecture]]. It is the oldest tumulus in Saitama Prefecture known to feature [[:en:Haniwa|haniwa]].
== Overview == Constructed atop the 90-meter-high summit of Raidenyama Hill in Ōya, the tumulus was built with earth heaped only on the uppermost tier of the rear circle, while other parts were carved from the natural terrain. The tumulus shape is similar to the [[:en:Otomeyama Kofun|Otomeyama Kofun]] in Nara Prefecture. Excavation in 1984 revealed a three-tiered mound structure, with paving stones on the summit of the front section and four concentric rows of haniwa encircling the tumulus. These haniwa are the oldest found in Saitama Prefecture. A square arrangement of haniwa was also discovered on the rear summit, suggesting the presence of a burial facility beneath. The burial facility is presumed to be either a clay-enclosed burial chamber or a wooden coffin burial.
The site was designated as part of the "Sanzenzuka Kofun Cluster" municipal historic site in 1956, and the excavated haniwa were designated as municipal tangible cultural properties in 2004.
Isao Masuda hypothesized that the origin of the [[:en:Sakitama Kofun Cluster|Sakitama Kofun Cluster]] lies in the Hiki region and identified Raidenyama Kofun as the tomb of Halfebi, the grandfather of {{ill|Owari no Omi|ja|乎獲居臣|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Owari no Omi|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}}.<ref>Kuraji Nakamura, "The Musashi Provincial Family Conflict and Large-Scale Mounds," [[:en:Saitama Prefectural Museum of History and Folklore|Saitama Prefectural Museum of History and Folklore]].</ref>
== Excavated Artifacts == [[File:雷電山古墳 (東松山市) 出土 円筒埴輪.JPG|thumb|180px|right|{{center|Cylindrical haniwa}}{{small|Displayed at the [[:en:Saitama Prefectural Museum of History and Folklore|Saitama Prefectural Museum of History and Folklore]].}}]] Artifacts include {{ill|morning glory-shaped haniwa|ja|朝顔形埴輪|1=Morning glory-shaped haniwa|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/morning glory-shaped haniwa|13=User:Immanuelle/Morning glory-shaped haniwa|qq=}}, cylindrical haniwa, and stand-shaped haniwa. These artifacts differ significantly from commonly known haniwa, as they lack brush marks typically found on their surfaces. This indicates a manufacturing technique closer to [[:en:Haji pottery|Haji pottery]]. Evidence of open firing, resulting in blackened surfaces, suggests that these haniwa were made in the early 5th century. These artifacts are currently on display at the [[:en:Saitama Prefectural Museum of History and Folklore|Saitama Prefectural Museum of History and Folklore]].
== Notes == {{Reflist}}
== References == * Hiroshi Shiono, *Kofun of Saitama: Hiki and Chichibu*, pp. 67–75, Sakitama Publishing, 2004. == See Also == * {{ill|List of kofun in Japan|ja|日本の古墳一覧|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/List of kofun in Japan|1=list of kofun|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11505809}} * {{ill|List of kofun in Saitama Prefecture|ja|埼玉県の古墳一覧|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/List of kofun in Saitama Prefecture|1=List of Kofun in Saitama Prefecture|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11426533}}

<!--interwikis from wikidata-->[[ja:雷電山古墳]] {{afc comment|Wikidata entry [[da:d:Q16768352]] Wikidata name: (none) [[User:Immanuelle|Immanuelle]] ([[User talk:Immanuelle|talk]]) 05:24, 12 June 2025 (UTC)}} {{afc comment|Wikidata entry (none) Wikidata name: (none) [[User:Immanuelle|Immanuelle]] ([[User talk:Immanuelle|talk]]) 08:55, 11 June 2025 (UTC)}} {{Infobox tumulus | name = Raidenyama Kofun | image = {{Image requested|cat=Higashimatsuyama}} | belonging_to = {{ill|Sanchinzuka Kofun Group|lt=Sanchinzuka Kofun Group|lt=Sanzenzuka Kofun Cluster|ja|三千塚古墳群|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Sanzenzuka Kofun Cluster|qq=}} | location = {{ill|Ōya, Higashimatsuyama|ja|東松山市大谷|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ōya, Higashimatsuyama|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}}, [[:en:Higashimatsuyama, Saitama|Higashimatsuyama]], [[:en:Saitama Prefecture|Saitama Prefecture]], Japan | coordinates = {{Coord|36|05|13.8|N|139|23|17.8|E|region:JP|display=inline,title}} | shape = {{ill|Scallop-shaped tumulus|ja|帆立貝形古墳|1=scallop-shaped kofun|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Scallop-shaped tumulus|13=User:Immanuelle/scallop-shaped kofun|comment=enwiki is a redirect}} | area = Total length 85m, height 7m, rear circle diameter 73m, front width 39m | construction_era = Early 5th century | artifacts = [[:en:Haniwa|haniwa]] | historic_site_designation = Included in Sanzenzuka Kofun Cluster; designated as a municipal historic site on February 6, 1956<ref name="Higashimatsuyama">{{Cite web|url=http://www.city.higashimatsuyama.lg.jp/kurashi/bunka_sports/bunka/bunkazai/shitei_bunkazai/1350459239838.html|title=List of Cultural Properties|publisher=Higashimatsuyama City|date=2021-07-02|accessdate=2021-11-29}}</ref> | tangible_cultural_properties = Excavated haniwa, designated on March 26, 2004<ref name="Higashimatsuyama"/> | map = {{Location map|Japan Saitama|label=Raidenyama Kofun|lat_deg=36|lat_min=05|lat_sec=13.8|lon_deg=139|lon_min=23|lon_sec=17.8|mark=帆立貝形古墳-北西.svg|marksize=28|float=center|border=none|position=bottom|width=200|caption=Location in Saitama Prefecture}} }}
'''Raidenyama Kofun''' (雷電山古墳) is a {{ill|scallop-shaped tumulus|ja|帆立貝形古墳|1=scallop-shaped kofun|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/scallop-shaped tumulus|13=User:Immanuelle/scallop-shaped kofun|comment=enwiki is a redirect}} located in the {{ill|Sanchinzuka Kofun Group|lt=Sanchinzuka Kofun Group|lt=Sanzenzuka Kofun Cluster|ja|三千塚古墳群|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Sanzenzuka Kofun Cluster|qq=}} in [[:en:Higashimatsuyama, Saitama|Higashimatsuyama]], [[:en:Saitama Prefecture|Saitama Prefecture]]. It is the oldest tumulus in Saitama Prefecture known to feature [[:en:Haniwa|haniwa]].
== Overview == Constructed atop the 90-meter-high summit of Raidenyama Hill in Ōya, the tumulus was built with earth heaped only on the uppermost tier of the rear circle, while other parts were carved from the natural terrain. The tumulus shape is similar to the [[:en:Otomeyama Kofun|Otomeyama Kofun]] in Nara Prefecture. Excavation in 1984 revealed a three-tiered mound structure, with paving stones on the summit of the front section and four concentric rows of haniwa encircling the tumulus. These haniwa are the oldest found in Saitama Prefecture. A square arrangement of haniwa was also discovered on the rear summit, suggesting the presence of a burial facility beneath. The burial facility is presumed to be either a clay-enclosed burial chamber or a wooden coffin burial.
The site was designated as part of the "Sanzenzuka Kofun Cluster" municipal historic site in 1956, and the excavated haniwa were designated as municipal tangible cultural properties in 2004.
Isao Masuda hypothesized that the origin of the [[:en:Sakitama Kofun Cluster|Sakitama Kofun Cluster]] lies in the Hiki region and identified Raidenyama Kofun as the tomb of Halfebi, the grandfather of {{ill|Owari no Omi|ja|乎獲居臣|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Owari no Omi|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}}.<ref>Kuraji Nakamura, "The Musashi Provincial Family Conflict and Large-Scale Mounds," [[:en:Saitama Prefectural Museum of History and Folklore|Saitama Prefectural Museum of History and Folklore]].</ref>
== Excavated Artifacts == [[File:雷電山古墳 (東松山市) 出土 円筒埴輪.JPG|thumb|180px|right|{{center|Cylindrical haniwa}}{{small|Displayed at the [[:en:Saitama Prefectural Museum of History and Folklore|Saitama Prefectural Museum of History and Folklore]].}}]] Artifacts include {{ill|morning glory-shaped haniwa|ja|朝顔形埴輪|1=Morning glory-shaped haniwa|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/morning glory-shaped haniwa|13=User:Immanuelle/Morning glory-shaped haniwa|qq=}}, cylindrical haniwa, and stand-shaped haniwa. These artifacts differ significantly from commonly known haniwa, as they lack brush marks typically found on their surfaces. This indicates a manufacturing technique closer to [[:en:Haji pottery|Haji pottery]]. Evidence of open firing, resulting in blackened surfaces, suggests that these haniwa were made in the early 5th century. These artifacts are currently on display at the [[:en:Saitama Prefectural Museum of History and Folklore|Saitama Prefectural Museum of History and Folklore]].
== Notes == {{Reflist}}
== References == * Hiroshi Shiono, *Kofun of Saitama: Hiki and Chichibu*, pp. 67–75, Sakitama Publishing, 2004. == See Also == * {{ill|List of kofun in Japan|ja|日本の古墳一覧|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/List of kofun in Japan|1=list of kofun|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11505809}} * {{ill|List of kofun in Saitama Prefecture|ja|埼玉県の古墳一覧|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/List of kofun in Saitama Prefecture|1=List of Kofun in Saitama Prefecture|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11426533}}
== External Links == * [http://kofun.info/kofun/312 Raidenyama Kofun] - Kofun Map * [http://www.city.higashimatsuyama.lg.jp/kurashi/bunka_sports/bunka/bunkazai/shitei_bunkazai/1350459239838.html List of Cultural Properties] - Higashimatsuyama City

designated as a municipal historic site on February 6, 1956<ref name="Higashimatsuyama">{{Cite web|url=http://www.city.higashimatsuyama.lg.jp/kurashi/bunka_sports/bunka/bunkazai/shitei_bunkazai/1350459239838.html|title=List of Cultural Properties|publisher=Higashimatsuyama City|date=2021-07-02|accessdate=2021-11-29}}</ref> | tangible_cultural_properties = Excavated haniwa, designated on March 26, 2004<ref name="Higashimatsuyama"/> | map = {{Location map|Japan Saitama|label=Raidenyama Kofun|lat_deg=36|lat_min=05|lat_sec=13.8|lon_deg=139|lon_min=23|lon_sec=17.8|mark=帆立貝形古墳-北西.svg|marksize=28|float=center|border=none|position=bottom|width=200|caption=Location in Saitama Prefecture}} }}
'''Raidenyama Kofun''' (雷電山古墳) is a {{ill|scallop-shaped tumulus|ja|帆立貝形古墳|1=scallop-shaped kofun|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/scallop-shaped tumulus|13=User:Immanuelle/scallop-shaped kofun|comment=enwiki is a redirect}} located in the {{ill|Sanchinzuka Kofun Group|lt=Sanchinzuka Kofun Group|lt=Sanzenzuka Kofun Cluster|ja|三千塚古墳群|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Sanzenzuka Kofun Cluster|qq=}} in [[:en:Higashimatsuyama, Saitama|Higashimatsuyama]], [[:en:Saitama Prefecture|Saitama Prefecture]]. It is the oldest tumulus in Saitama Prefecture known to feature [[:en:Haniwa|haniwa]].
== Overview == Constructed atop the 90-meter-high summit of Raidenyama Hill in Ōya, the tumulus was built with earth heaped only on the uppermost tier of the rear circle, while other parts were carved from the natural terrain. The tumulus shape is similar to the [[:en:Otomeyama Kofun|Otomeyama Kofun]] in Nara Prefecture. Excavation in 1984 revealed a three-tiered mound structure, with paving stones on the summit of the front section and four concentric rows of haniwa encircling the tumulus. These haniwa are the oldest found in Saitama Prefecture. A square arrangement of haniwa was also discovered on the rear summit, suggesting the presence of a burial facility beneath. The burial facility is presumed to be either a clay-enclosed burial chamber or a wooden coffin burial.
The site was designated as part of the "Sanzenzuka Kofun Cluster" municipal historic site in 1956, and the excavated haniwa were designated as municipal tangible cultural properties in 2004.
Isao Masuda hypothesized that the origin of the [[:en:Sakitama Kofun Cluster|Sakitama Kofun Cluster]] lies in the Hiki region and identified Raidenyama Kofun as the tomb of Halfebi, the grandfather of {{ill|Owari no Omi|ja|乎獲居臣|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Owari no Omi|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}}.<ref>Kuraji Nakamura, "The Musashi Provincial Family Conflict and Large-Scale Mounds," [[:en:Saitama Prefectural Museum of History and Folklore|Saitama Prefectural Museum of History and Folklore]].</ref>
== Excavated Artifacts == [[File:雷電山古墳 (東松山市) 出土 円筒埴輪.JPG|thumb|180px|right|{{center|Cylindrical haniwa}}{{small|Displayed at the [[:en:Saitama Prefectural Museum of History and Folklore|Saitama Prefectural Museum of History and Folklore]].}}]] Artifacts include {{ill|morning glory-shaped haniwa|ja|朝顔形埴輪|1=Morning glory-shaped haniwa|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/morning glory-shaped haniwa|13=User:Immanuelle/Morning glory-shaped haniwa|qq=}}, cylindrical haniwa, and stand-shaped haniwa. These artifacts differ significantly from commonly known haniwa, as they lack brush marks typically found on their surfaces. This indicates a manufacturing technique closer to [[:en:Haji pottery|Haji pottery]]. Evidence of open firing, resulting in blackened surfaces, suggests that these haniwa were made in the early 5th century. These artifacts are currently on display at the [[:en:Saitama Prefectural Museum of History and Folklore|Saitama Prefectural Museum of History and Folklore]].
== Notes == {{Reflist}}
== References == * Hiroshi Shiono, *Kofun of Saitama: Hiki and Chichibu*, pp. 67–75, Sakitama Publishing, 2004. == See Also == * {{ill|List of kofun in Japan|ja|日本の古墳一覧|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/List of kofun in Japan|1=list of kofun|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11505809}} * {{ill|List of kofun in Saitama Prefecture|ja|埼玉県の古墳一覧|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/List of kofun in Saitama Prefecture|1=List of Kofun in Saitama Prefecture|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11426533}}
== External Links == * [http://kofun.info/kofun/312 Raidenyama Kofun] - Kofun Map * [http://www.city.higashimatsuyama.lg.jp/kurashi/bunka_sports/bunka/bunkazai/shitei_bunkazai/1350459239838.html List of Cultural Properties] - Higashimatsuyama City

designated as a municipal historic site on February 6, 1956<ref name="Higashimatsuyama">{{Cite web|url=http://www.city.higashimatsuyama.lg.jp/kurashi/bunka_sports/bunka/bunkazai/shitei_bunkazai/1350459239838.html|title=List of Cultural Properties|publisher=Higashimatsuyama City|date=2021-07-02|accessdate=2021-11-29}}</ref> | tangible_cultural_properties = Excavated haniwa, designated on March 26, 2004<ref name="Higashimatsuyama"/> | map = {{Location map|Japan Saitama|label=Raidenyama Kofun|lat_deg=36|lat_min=05|lat_sec=13.8|lon_deg=139|lon_min=23|lon_sec=17.8|mark=帆立貝形古墳-北西.svg|marksize=28|float=center|border=none|position=bottom|width=200|caption=Location in Saitama Prefecture}} }}
'''Raidenyama Kofun''' (雷電山古墳) is a {{ill|scallop-shaped tumulus|ja|帆立貝形古墳|1=scallop-shaped kofun|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/scallop-shaped tumulus|13=User:Immanuelle/scallop-shaped kofun|comment=enwiki is a redirect}} located in the {{ill|Sanchinzuka Kofun Group|lt=Sanchinzuka Kofun Group|lt=Sanzenzuka Kofun Cluster|ja|三千塚古墳群|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Sanzenzuka Kofun Cluster|qq=}} in [[:en:Higashimatsuyama, Saitama|Higashimatsuyama]], [[:en:Saitama Prefecture|Saitama Prefecture]]. It is the oldest tumulus in Saitama Prefecture known to feature [[:en:Haniwa|haniwa]].
== Overview == Constructed atop the 90-meter-high summit of Raidenyama Hill in Ōya, the tumulus was built with earth heaped only on the uppermost tier of the rear circle, while other parts were carved from the natural terrain. The tumulus shape is similar to the [[:en:Otomeyama Kofun|Otomeyama Kofun]] in Nara Prefecture. Excavation in 1984 revealed a three-tiered mound structure, with paving stones on the summit of the front section and four concentric rows of haniwa encircling the tumulus. These haniwa are the oldest found in Saitama Prefecture. A square arrangement of haniwa was also discovered on the rear summit, suggesting the presence of a burial facility beneath. The burial facility is presumed to be either a clay-enclosed burial chamber or a wooden coffin burial.
The site was designated as part of the "Sanzenzuka Kofun Cluster" municipal historic site in 1956, and the excavated haniwa were designated as municipal tangible cultural properties in 2004.
Isao Masuda hypothesized that the origin of the [[:en:Sakitama Kofun Cluster|Sakitama Kofun Cluster]] lies in the Hiki region and identified Raidenyama Kofun as the tomb of Halfebi, the grandfather of {{ill|Owari no Omi|ja|乎獲居臣|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Owari no Omi|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}}.<ref>Kuraji Nakamura, "The Musashi Provincial Family Conflict and Large-Scale Mounds," [[:en:Saitama Prefectural Museum of History and Folklore|Saitama Prefectural Museum of History and Folklore]].</ref>
== Excavated Artifacts == [[File:雷電山古墳 (東松山市) 出土 円筒埴輪.JPG|thumb|180px|right|{{center|Cylindrical haniwa}}{{small|Displayed at the [[:en:Saitama Prefectural Museum of History and Folklore|Saitama Prefectural Museum of History and Folklore]].}}]] Artifacts include {{ill|morning glory-shaped haniwa|ja|朝顔形埴輪|1=Morning glory-shaped haniwa|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/morning glory-shaped haniwa|13=User:Immanuelle/Morning glory-shaped haniwa|qq=}}, cylindrical haniwa, and stand-shaped haniwa. These artifacts differ significantly from commonly known haniwa, as they lack brush marks typically found on their surfaces. This indicates a manufacturing technique closer to [[:en:Haji pottery|Haji pottery]]. Evidence of open firing, resulting in blackened surfaces, suggests that these haniwa were made in the early 5th century. These artifacts are currently on display at the [[:en:Saitama Prefectural Museum of History and Folklore|Saitama Prefectural Museum of History and Folklore]].
== Notes == {{Reflist}}
== References == * Hiroshi Shiono, *Kofun of Saitama: Hiki and Chichibu*, pp. 67–75, Sakitama Publishing, 2004. == See Also == * {{ill|List of kofun in Japan|ja|日本の古墳一覧|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/List of kofun in Japan|1=list of kofun|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11505809}} * {{ill|List of kofun in Saitama Prefecture|ja|埼玉県の古墳一覧|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/List of kofun in Saitama Prefecture|1=List of Kofun in Saitama Prefecture|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11426533}}
== External Links == * [http://kofun.info/kofun/312 Raidenyama Kofun] - Kofun Map * [http://www.city.higashimatsuyama.lg.jp/kurashi/bunka_sports/bunka/bunkazai/shitei_bunkazai/1350459239838.html List of Cultural Properties] - Higashimatsuyama City
{{translated page|ja|雷電山古墳 (東松山市)|version=98330746|comment=Imported full ja history}}

designated as a municipal historic site on February 6, 1956<ref name="Higashimatsuyama">{{Cite web|url=http://www.city.higashimatsuyama.lg.jp/kurashi/bunka_sports/bunka/bunkazai/shitei_bunkazai/1350459239838.html|title=List of Cultural Properties|publisher=Higashimatsuyama City|date=2021-07-02|accessdate=2021-11-29}}</ref> | tangible_cultural_properties = Excavated haniwa, designated on March 26, 2004<ref name="Higashimatsuyama"/> | map = {{Location map|Japan Saitama|label=Raidenyama Kofun|lat_deg=36|lat_min=05|lat_sec=13.8|lon_deg=139|lon_min=23|lon_sec=17.8|mark=帆立貝形古墳-北西.svg|marksize=28|float=center|border=none|position=bottom|width=200|caption=Location in Saitama Prefecture}} }}
'''Raidenyama Kofun''' (雷電山古墳) is a {{ill|scallop-shaped tumulus|ja|帆立貝形古墳|1=scallop-shaped kofun|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/scallop-shaped tumulus|13=User:Immanuelle/scallop-shaped kofun|comment=enwiki is a redirect}} located in the {{ill|Sanchinzuka Kofun Group|lt=Sanchinzuka Kofun Group|lt=Sanzenzuka Kofun Cluster|ja|三千塚古墳群|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Sanzenzuka Kofun Cluster|qq=}} in {{ill|Higashimatsuyama, Saitama|en|Higashimatsuyama, Saitama|WD=Q823907|lt=Higashimatsuyama}}, {{ill|Saitama Prefecture|en|Saitama Prefecture|WD=Q128186|lt=Saitama Prefecture}}. It is the oldest tumulus in Saitama Prefecture known to feature {{ill|Haniwa|en|Haniwa|WD=Q849376|lt=haniwa}}.
== Overview == Constructed atop the 90-meter-high summit of Raidenyama Hill in Ōya, the tumulus was built with earth heaped only on the uppermost tier of the rear circle, while other parts were carved from the natural terrain. The tumulus shape is similar to the {{ill|Otomeyama Kofun|en|Otomeyama Kofun|WD=Q11369896|lt=Otomeyama Kofun}} in Nara Prefecture. Excavation in 1984 revealed a three-tiered mound structure, with paving stones on the summit of the front section and four concentric rows of haniwa encircling the tumulus. These haniwa are the oldest found in Saitama Prefecture. A square arrangement of haniwa was also discovered on the rear summit, suggesting the presence of a burial facility beneath. The burial facility is presumed to be either a clay-enclosed burial chamber or a wooden coffin burial.
The site was designated as part of the "Sanzenzuka Kofun Cluster" municipal historic site in 1956, and the excavated haniwa were designated as municipal tangible cultural properties in 2004.
Isao Masuda hypothesized that the origin of the {{ill|Sakitama Kofun Cluster|en|Sakitama Kofun Cluster|WD=Q2213580|lt=Sakitama Kofun Cluster}} lies in the Hiki region and identified Raidenyama Kofun as the tomb of Halfebi, the grandfather of {{ill|Owari no Omi|ja|乎獲居臣|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Owari no Omi|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}}.<ref>Kuraji Nakamura, "The Musashi Provincial Family Conflict and Large-Scale Mounds," {{ill|Saitama Prefectural Museum of History and Folklore|en|Saitama Prefectural Museum of History and Folklore|WD=Q11248611|lt=Saitama Prefectural Museum of History and Folklore}}.</ref>
== Excavated Artifacts == [[File:雷電山古墳 (東松山市) 出土 円筒埴輪.JPG|thumb|180px|right|{{center|Cylindrical haniwa}}{{small|Displayed at the {{ill|Saitama Prefectural Museum of History and Folklore|en|Saitama Prefectural Museum of History and Folklore|WD=Q11248611|lt=Saitama Prefectural Museum of History and Folklore}}.}}]] Artifacts include {{ill|morning glory-shaped haniwa|ja|朝顔形埴輪|1=Morning glory-shaped haniwa|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/morning glory-shaped haniwa|13=User:Immanuelle/Morning glory-shaped haniwa|qq=}}, cylindrical haniwa, and stand-shaped haniwa. These artifacts differ significantly from commonly known haniwa, as they lack brush marks typically found on their surfaces. This indicates a manufacturing technique closer to {{ill|Haji pottery|en|Haji pottery|WD=Q5639938|lt=Haji pottery}}. Evidence of open firing, resulting in blackened surfaces, suggests that these haniwa were made in the early 5th century. These artifacts are currently on display at the {{ill|Saitama Prefectural Museum of History and Folklore|en|Saitama Prefectural Museum of History and Folklore|WD=Q11248611|lt=Saitama Prefectural Museum of History and Folklore}}.
== Notes == {{Reflist}}
== References == * Hiroshi Shiono, *Kofun of Saitama: Hiki and Chichibu*, pp. 67–75, Sakitama Publishing, 2004. == See Also == * {{ill|List of kofun in Japan|ja|日本の古墳一覧|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/List of kofun in Japan|1=list of kofun|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11505809}} * {{ill|List of kofun in Saitama Prefecture|ja|埼玉県の古墳一覧|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/List of kofun in Saitama Prefecture|1=List of Kofun in Saitama Prefecture|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11426533}}
== External Links == * [http://kofun.info/kofun/312 Raidenyama Kofun] - Kofun Map * [http://www.city.higashimatsuyama.lg.jp/kurashi/bunka_sports/bunka/bunkazai/shitei_bunkazai/1350459239838.html List of Cultural Properties] - Higashimatsuyama City
{{translated page|ja|雷電山古墳 (東松山市)|version=98330746|comment=Imported full ja history}}
[[ja:雷電山古墳]]

<!--interwikis from wikidata-->[[ja:雷電山古墳]] <!--interwikis from wikidata-->[[ja:雷電山古墳]] <!--interwikis from wikidata-->[[ja:雷電山古墳]]
{{Infobox tumulus | name = Raidenyama Kofun | image = {{Image requested|cat=Higashimatsuyama}} | belonging_to = {{ill|Sanchinzuka Kofun Group|lt=Sanchinzuka Kofun Group|lt=Sanzenzuka Kofun Cluster|ja|三千塚古墳群|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Sanzenzuka Kofun Cluster|qq=}} | location = {{ill|Ōya, Higashimatsuyama|ja|東松山市大谷|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ōya, Higashimatsuyama|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}}, {{ill|Higashimatsuyama, Saitama|en|Higashimatsuyama, Saitama|WD=Q823907|lt=Higashimatsuyama}}, {{ill|Saitama Prefecture|en|Saitama Prefecture|WD=Q128186|lt=Saitama Prefecture}}, Japan | coordinates = {{Coord|36|05|13.8|N|139|23|17.8|E|region:JP|display=inline,title}} | shape = {{ill|Scallop-shaped tumulus|ja|帆立貝形古墳|1=scallop-shaped kofun|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Scallop-shaped tumulus|13=User:Immanuelle/scallop-shaped kofun|comment=enwiki is a redirect}} | area = Total length 85m, height 7m, rear circle diameter 73m, front width 39m | construction_era = Early 5th century | artifacts = {{ill|Haniwa|en|Haniwa|WD=Q849376|lt=haniwa}} | historic_site_designation = Included in Sanzenzuka Kofun Cluster; designated as a municipal historic site on February 6, 1956<ref name="Higashimatsuyama">{{Cite web|url=http://www.city.higashimatsuyama.lg.jp/kurashi/bunka_sports/bunka/bunkazai/shitei_bunkazai/1350459239838.html|title=List of Cultural Properties|publisher=Higashimatsuyama City|date=2021-07-02|accessdate=2021-11-29}}</ref> | tangible_cultural_properties = Excavated haniwa, designated on March 26, 2004<ref name="Higashimatsuyama"/> | map = {{Location map|Japan Saitama|label=Raidenyama Kofun|lat_deg=36|lat_min=05|lat_sec=13.8|lon_deg=139|lon_min=23|lon_sec=17.8|mark=帆立貝形古墳-北西.svg|marksize=28|float=center|border=none|position=bottom|width=200|caption=Location in Saitama Prefecture}} }}
'''Raidenyama Kofun''' (雷電山古墳) is a {{ill|scallop-shaped tumulus|ja|帆立貝形古墳|1=scallop-shaped kofun|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/scallop-shaped tumulus|13=User:Immanuelle/scallop-shaped kofun|comment=enwiki is a redirect}} located in the {{ill|Sanchinzuka Kofun Group|lt=Sanchinzuka Kofun Group|lt=Sanzenzuka Kofun Cluster|ja|三千塚古墳群|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Sanzenzuka Kofun Cluster|qq=}} in {{ill|Higashimatsuyama, Saitama|en|Higashimatsuyama, Saitama|WD=Q823907|lt=Higashimatsuyama}}, {{ill|Saitama Prefecture|en|Saitama Prefecture|WD=Q128186|lt=Saitama Prefecture}}. It is the oldest tumulus in Saitama Prefecture known to feature {{ill|Haniwa|en|Haniwa|WD=Q849376|lt=haniwa}}.
== Overview == Constructed atop the 90-meter-high summit of Raidenyama Hill in Ōya, the tumulus was built with earth heaped only on the uppermost tier of the rear circle, while other parts were carved from the natural terrain. The tumulus shape is similar to the {{ill|Otomeyama Kofun|en|Otomeyama Kofun|WD=Q11369896|lt=Otomeyama Kofun}} in Nara Prefecture. Excavation in 1984 revealed a three-tiered mound structure, with paving stones on the summit of the front section and four concentric rows of haniwa encircling the tumulus. These haniwa are the oldest found in Saitama Prefecture. A square arrangement of haniwa was also discovered on the rear summit, suggesting the presence of a burial facility beneath. The burial facility is presumed to be either a clay-enclosed burial chamber or a wooden coffin burial.
The site was designated as part of the "Sanzenzuka Kofun Cluster" municipal historic site in 1956, and the excavated haniwa were designated as municipal tangible cultural properties in 2004.
Isao Masuda hypothesized that the origin of the {{ill|Sakitama Kofun Cluster|en|Sakitama Kofun Cluster|WD=Q2213580|lt=Sakitama Kofun Cluster}} lies in the Hiki region and identified Raidenyama Kofun as the tomb of Halfebi, the grandfather of {{ill|Owari no Omi|ja|乎獲居臣|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Owari no Omi|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}}.<ref>Kuraji Nakamura, "The Musashi Provincial Family Conflict and Large-Scale Mounds," {{ill|Saitama Prefectural Museum of History and Folklore|en|Saitama Prefectural Museum of History and Folklore|WD=Q11248611|lt=Saitama Prefectural Museum of History and Folklore}}.</ref>
== Excavated Artifacts == [[File:雷電山古墳 (東松山市) 出土 円筒埴輪.JPG|thumb|180px|right|{{center|Cylindrical haniwa}}{{small|Displayed at the {{ill|Saitama Prefectural Museum of History and Folklore|en|Saitama Prefectural Museum of History and Folklore|WD=Q11248611|lt=Saitama Prefectural Museum of History and Folklore}}.}}]] Artifacts include {{ill|morning glory-shaped haniwa|ja|朝顔形埴輪|1=Morning glory-shaped haniwa|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/morning glory-shaped haniwa|13=User:Immanuelle/Morning glory-shaped haniwa|qq=}}, cylindrical haniwa, and stand-shaped haniwa. These artifacts differ significantly from commonly known haniwa, as they lack brush marks typically found on their surfaces. This indicates a manufacturing technique closer to {{ill|Haji pottery|en|Haji pottery|WD=Q5639938|lt=Haji pottery}}. Evidence of open firing, resulting in blackened surfaces, suggests that these haniwa were made in the early 5th century. These artifacts are currently on display at the {{ill|Saitama Prefectural Museum of History and Folklore|en|Saitama Prefectural Museum of History and Folklore|WD=Q11248611|lt=Saitama Prefectural Museum of History and Folklore}}.
== Notes == {{Reflist}}
== References == * Hiroshi Shiono, *Kofun of Saitama: Hiki and Chichibu*, pp. 67–75, Sakitama Publishing, 2004. == See Also == * {{ill|List of kofun in Japan|ja|日本の古墳一覧|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/List of kofun in Japan|1=list of kofun|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11505809}} * {{ill|List of kofun in Saitama Prefecture|ja|埼玉県の古墳一覧|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/List of kofun in Saitama Prefecture|1=List of Kofun in Saitama Prefecture|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11426533}}
== External Links == * [http://kofun.info/kofun/312 Raidenyama Kofun] - Kofun Map * [http://www.city.higashimatsuyama.lg.jp/kurashi/bunka_sports/bunka/bunkazai/shitei_bunkazai/1350459239838.html List of Cultural Properties] - Higashimatsuyama City
{{translated page|ja|雷電山古墳 (東松山市)|version=98330746|comment=Imported full ja history}}

{{Infobox tumulus | name = Raidenyama Kofun | image = {{Image requested|cat=Higashimatsuyama}} | belonging_to = {{ill|Sanchinzuka Kofun Group|lt=Sanchinzuka Kofun Group|lt=Sanzenzuka Kofun Cluster|ja|三千塚古墳群|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Sanzenzuka Kofun Cluster|qq=}} | location = {{ill|Ōya, Higashimatsuyama|ja|東松山市大谷|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ōya, Higashimatsuyama|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}}, {{ill|Higashimatsuyama, Saitama|en|Higashimatsuyama, Saitama|WD=Q823907|lt=Higashimatsuyama|1=Higashimatsuyama, Saitama}}, {{ill|Saitama Prefecture|en|Saitama Prefecture|WD=Q128186|lt=Saitama Prefecture|1=Saitama Prefecture}}, Japan | coordinates = {{Coord|36|05|13.8|N|139|23|17.8|E|region:JP|display=inline,title}} | shape = {{ill|Scallop-shaped tumulus|ja|帆立貝形古墳|1=scallop-shaped kofun|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Scallop-shaped tumulus|13=User:Immanuelle/scallop-shaped kofun|comment=enwiki is a redirect}} | area = Total length 85m, height 7m, rear circle diameter 73m, front width 39m | construction_era = Early 5th century | artifacts = {{ill|Haniwa|en|Haniwa|WD=Q849376|lt=haniwa|1=Haniwa}} | historic_site_designation = Included in Sanzenzuka Kofun Cluster; designated as a municipal historic site on February 6, 1956<ref name="Higashimatsuyama">{{Cite web|url=http://www.city.higashimatsuyama.lg.jp/kurashi/bunka_sports/bunka/bunkazai/shitei_bunkazai/1350459239838.html|title=List of Cultural Properties|publisher=Higashimatsuyama City|date=2021-07-02|accessdate=2021-11-29}}</ref> | tangible_cultural_properties = Excavated haniwa, designated on March 26, 2004<ref name="Higashimatsuyama"/> | map = {{Location map|Japan Saitama|label=Raidenyama Kofun|lat_deg=36|lat_min=05|lat_sec=13.8|lon_deg=139|lon_min=23|lon_sec=17.8|mark=帆立貝形古墳-北西.svg|marksize=28|float=center|border=none|position=bottom|width=200|caption=Location in Saitama Prefecture}} }}
'''Raidenyama Kofun''' (雷電山古墳) is a {{ill|scallop-shaped tumulus|ja|帆立貝形古墳|1=scallop-shaped kofun|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/scallop-shaped tumulus|13=User:Immanuelle/scallop-shaped kofun|comment=enwiki is a redirect}} located in the {{ill|Sanchinzuka Kofun Group|lt=Sanchinzuka Kofun Group|lt=Sanzenzuka Kofun Cluster|ja|三千塚古墳群|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Sanzenzuka Kofun Cluster|qq=}} in {{ill|Higashimatsuyama, Saitama|en|Higashimatsuyama, Saitama|WD=Q823907|lt=Higashimatsuyama|1=Higashimatsuyama, Saitama}}, {{ill|Saitama Prefecture|en|Saitama Prefecture|WD=Q128186|lt=Saitama Prefecture|1=Saitama Prefecture}}. It is the oldest tumulus in Saitama Prefecture known to feature {{ill|Haniwa|en|Haniwa|WD=Q849376|lt=haniwa|1=Haniwa}}.
== Overview == Constructed atop the 90-meter-high summit of Raidenyama Hill in Ōya, the tumulus was built with earth heaped only on the uppermost tier of the rear circle, while other parts were carved from the natural terrain. The tumulus shape is similar to the {{ill|Otomeyama Kofun|en|Otomeyama Kofun|WD=Q11369896|lt=Otomeyama Kofun|1=Otomeyama Kofun}} in Nara Prefecture. Excavation in 1984 revealed a three-tiered mound structure, with paving stones on the summit of the front section and four concentric rows of haniwa encircling the tumulus. These haniwa are the oldest found in Saitama Prefecture. A square arrangement of haniwa was also discovered on the rear summit, suggesting the presence of a burial facility beneath. The burial facility is presumed to be either a clay-enclosed burial chamber or a wooden coffin burial.
The site was designated as part of the "Sanzenzuka Kofun Cluster" municipal historic site in 1956, and the excavated haniwa were designated as municipal tangible cultural properties in 2004.
Isao Masuda hypothesized that the origin of the {{ill|Sakitama Kofun Cluster|en|Sakitama Kofun Cluster|WD=Q2213580|lt=Sakitama Kofun Cluster|1=Sakitama Kofun Cluster}} lies in the Hiki region and identified Raidenyama Kofun as the tomb of Halfebi, the grandfather of {{ill|Owari no Omi|ja|乎獲居臣|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Owari no Omi|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}}.<ref>Kuraji Nakamura, "The Musashi Provincial Family Conflict and Large-Scale Mounds," {{ill|Saitama Prefectural Museum of History and Folklore|en|Saitama Prefectural Museum of History and Folklore|WD=Q11248611|lt=Saitama Prefectural Museum of History and Folklore|1=Saitama Prefectural Museum of History and Folklore}}.</ref>
== Excavated Artifacts == [[File:雷電山古墳 (東松山市) 出土 円筒埴輪.JPG|thumb|180px|right|{{center|Cylindrical haniwa}}{{small|Displayed at the {{ill|Saitama Prefectural Museum of History and Folklore|en|Saitama Prefectural Museum of History and Folklore|WD=Q11248611|lt=Saitama Prefectural Museum of History and Folklore|1=Saitama Prefectural Museum of History and Folklore}}.}}]] Artifacts include {{ill|morning glory-shaped haniwa|ja|朝顔形埴輪|1=Morning glory-shaped haniwa|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/morning glory-shaped haniwa|13=User:Immanuelle/Morning glory-shaped haniwa|qq=}}, cylindrical haniwa, and stand-shaped haniwa. These artifacts differ significantly from commonly known haniwa, as they lack brush marks typically found on their surfaces. This indicates a manufacturing technique closer to {{ill|Haji pottery|en|Haji pottery|WD=Q5639938|lt=Haji pottery|1=Haji pottery}}. Evidence of open firing, resulting in blackened surfaces, suggests that these haniwa were made in the early 5th century. These artifacts are currently on display at the {{ill|Saitama Prefectural Museum of History and Folklore|en|Saitama Prefectural Museum of History and Folklore|WD=Q11248611|lt=Saitama Prefectural Museum of History and Folklore|1=Saitama Prefectural Museum of History and Folklore}}.
== Notes == {{Reflist}}
== References == * Hiroshi Shiono, *Kofun of Saitama: Hiki and Chichibu*, pp. 67–75, Sakitama Publishing, 2004. == See Also == * {{ill|List of kofun in Japan|ja|日本の古墳一覧|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/List of kofun in Japan|1=list of kofun|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11505809}} * {{ill|List of kofun in Saitama Prefecture|ja|埼玉県の古墳一覧|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/List of kofun in Saitama Prefecture|1=list of Kofun in Saitama Prefecture|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11426533}}
== External Links == * [http://kofun.info/kofun/312 Raidenyama Kofun] - Kofun Map * [http://www.city.higashimatsuyama.lg.jp/kurashi/bunka_sports/bunka/bunkazai/shitei_bunkazai/1350459239838.html List of Cultural Properties] - Higashimatsuyama City
{{translated page|ja|雷電山古墳 (東松山市)|version=98330746|comment=Imported full ja history}}
